If iOS/Android needs grow heavier than what this SDK covers, Capacitor (web→mobile) or Tauri 2 (Rust-native) are documented alternatives in `external-research.md`.\n\n---\n\n## C. Existing tools to reuse\n\n### `konard/broadcast`\n\n- **Language:** JavaScript (Bun).\n- **What it does:** Multi-platform message broadcasting CLI for Telegram channels, VK walls, X.com. Modular per-platform broadcasters and dual Telegram (Bot + User) auth.\n- **Use in Meta Sovereign:** Outbound (export/post) side of the unified broadcasting UI (R-B4).\n\n### `konard/vk-bot`\n\n- **Language:** JavaScript.\n- **What it does:** Small VK bot using the Kate Mobile token auth flow.\n- **Use in Meta Sovereign:** Reference implementation for VK token bootstrap and message handling loops (R-E1).\n\n### `konard/vk`\n\n- **Language:** Shell.\n- **What it does:** \"VK automation for personal auditory growth\" — shell scripts plus Selenium-based automatic VK token refresh.\n- **Use in Meta Sovereign:** Headless token-refresh automation that can keep long-running personal VK importers authenticated (R-E1).\n\n### `konard/vk-export`\n\n- **Language:** Rust (with JS counterpart).\n- **What it does:** Parses exported VK private message HTML archives into JSON.\n- **Use in Meta Sovereign:** Direct importer for migrating archived VK conversations into the personal CRM (R-A1, R-E1). Already Rust — fits the \"pure Rust stack\" goal.\n\n### `konard/vk-browser`\n\n- **Language:** HTML/JS.\n- **What it does:** Pure-browser React.js VK bot doing OAuth (Kate Mobile) + long polling, no backend; deployable on GitHub Pages.\n- **Use in Meta Sovereign:** Demonstrates a fully client-side, server-less VK message ingestion path (R-E1, R-F4). Privacy-friendly because nothing leaves the browser.\n\n### `konard/telegram-bot`\n\n- **Language:** TypeScript.\n- **What it does:** Exports Telegram chat history (using a user account, not a bot) to Markdown + JSON + media files; partitions large exports; uses `lino-arguments`.\n- **Use in Meta Sovereign:** Primary Telegram importer (R-E2, R-A1).\n\n### `konard/follow`\n\n- **Language:** JavaScript (Bun).\n- **What it does:** CLI tools to list/manage VK group chats and join Telegram groups/channels.\n- **Use in Meta Sovereign:** Discovery side — enumerating user's group chats so they can be ingested (R-D1, R-E1, R-E2).\n\n### `konard/github-pages-telegram-mini-app`\n\n- **Language:** JavaScript (React + Vite).\n- **What it does:** Telegram Mini App template hosted on GitHub Pages, with Telegram SDK theme/user integration and Actions deployment.\n- **Use in Meta Sovereign:** Optional starter for a Telegram Mini App front-end of the meta profile (auth, theming, mobile UI). Supplementary to R-G3.\n\n### `konard/telegramify-markdown`\n\n- **Language:** Python (originally TypeScript port available).\n- **What it does:** Converts standard Markdown to Telegram MarkdownV2, handling escaping, headings, lists, tables, code, spoilers.\n- **Use in Meta Sovereign:** Render personal notes/exports back into Telegram cleanly when broadcasting/replying (R-B4, R-E2).\n\n---\n\n## D. UI quality references\n\n### `drklo/telegram` and `telegramdesktop/tdesktop`\n\n- The issue cites these two clients as the **quality bar** for the chat UI (R-B1). They are not dependencies — they're benchmarks. The interaction model (channel list, message-grouping, reactions, search, pinned messages, threaded replies) is what we replicate.\n\n### `n8n.io`\n\n- Cited as the **quality bar** for the automation node graph (R-C3). We do not depend on n8n; we build a similar canvas using Rete.js or React Flow (see `external-research.md`).\n\n---\n\n## E. Reference projects (UX inspiration)\n\n### `link-assistant/operator`\n\n- **Language:** JavaScript.\n- **What it does:** React UI concept for an \"infinite queue\" of cards/chats (humans + AIs from Telegram/VK/X) with two actions: DONE / NEXT, optimised for focus and decision-making.\n- **Use in Meta Sovereign:** Direct UX inspiration for the operator/inbox view (R-B3).\n\n---\n\n## F. Testing\n\n### `link-foundation/browser-commander`\n\n- **Language:** JavaScript (also Rust, Python).\n- **What it does:** Universal browser-automation library with a unified API across engines focused on \"stoppable page triggers\" via a page state machine (LOADING / WORKING).\n- **Use in Meta Sovereign:** Drives e2e tests (R-H4) and headless scraping for sites without official APIs (R-E5, R-E6).\n\n---\n\n## G. CI/CD baselines\n\n### `link-foundation/js-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template`\n\n- This repository was bootstrapped from this template (see `package.json` description and existing CHANGELOG).\n- **Use in Meta Sovereign:** Source of all current CI/CD jobs (R-H5).\n\n### `link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template`\n\n- The Rust counterpart with rustfmt, Clippy, llvm-cov, fragment-based changelog, crates.io OIDC publishing.\n- **Use in Meta Sovereign:** Will be applied to the pure-Rust stack (R-G2) and to the Rust+WASM heavy-workload crate (R-G1).\n\n---\n\n## H. Summary table\n\n| Concern | Library / Project | Status |\n| ------- | ----------------- | ------ |\n| Text data format | `links-notation`, `lino-objects-codec` | Linked from issue |\n| Binary data store | `doublets-rs`, `doublets-web`, `link-cli` | Linked from issue |\n| CLI args | `lino-arguments` | Linked from issue |\n| Cross-platform shell | `deep-foundation/sdk` | Linked from issue |\n| VK ingestion | `konard/vk*`, `konard/follow` | Linked from issue |\n| Telegram ingestion | `konard/telegram-bot`, `konard/follow`, `konard/telegramify-markdown` | Linked from issue |\n| Outbound broadcast | `konard/broadcast` | Linked from issue |\n| Operator UX inspiration | `link-assistant/operator` | Linked from issue |\n| Chat UI quality bar | `drklo/telegram`, `telegramdesktop/tdesktop` | Linked from issue |\n| Automation graph quality bar | `n8n.io` | Linked from issue |\n| E2e tests | `browser-commander` | Linked from issue |\n| CI/CD baselines | JS + Rust ai-pipeline templates | Linked from issue |\n| Local-first sync (CRDT) | Yjs / Automerge | External research — `external-research.md` |\n| Unified messaging legality | Beeper / Matrix / mautrix | External research |\n| Encrypted-at-rest | SQLCipher, libsodium, age | External research |\n| Browser SQLite | sqlite3.wasm + OPFS, wa-sqlite | External research |\n| Node editor | Rete.js v2 (preferred), React Flow | External research |\n| Fuzzy search | Fuse.js + MiniSearch | External research |\n| Pattern synthesis research | REGAE, SplitRegex, Ohm-JS | External research |\n| Mobile / desktop | Tauri 2 (alt), Capacitor (alt) | External research |\n",

[2026-05-01T08:51:02.731Z] [INFO]           "content": "# External Research — Issue #1\n\nThe issue prescribes a number of upstream libraries (catalogued in `components.md`). For everything *not* prescribed — local-first principles, CRDTs, unified messaging legality, encrypted-at-rest, pattern-synthesis literature, node editors, fuzzy search, browser SQLite, cross-platform shells — this document captures the publicly available information that informed the prototype's design.\n\nAll sources are public web pages. GitHub-hosted upstreams are listed in `components.md` instead.\n\n## 1. Local-first software principles\n\nThe seminal essay *\"Local-first software: You own your data, in spite of the cloud\"* (Kleppmann, Wiggins, van Hardenberg, McGranaghan; Ink & Switch, 2019) defines **seven ideals**: no spinners, multi-device, offline, collaboration, longevity, privacy, ownership. CRDTs are positioned as the foundational data structure for realising these ideals.\n\n- Essay: <https://www.inkandswitch.com/essay/local-first/>\n- Talk write-up: <https://martin.kleppmann.com/2019/10/23/local-first-at-onward.html>\n\nMeta Sovereign maps directly onto the seven ideals — every requirement in `requirements.md` lands in one of them.\n\n## 2. CRDTs and sync libraries\n\nTwo production-grade CRDT libraries dominate the JS ecosystem:\n\n| Library | Strengths | Weaknesses |\n| ------- | --------- | ---------- |\n| **Yjs** | Fastest CRDT for collaborative text; YATA + binary encoding; mature WebRTC/WebSocket adapters. | Lower-level model — JSON document semantics are looser than Automerge's. |\n| **Automerge** | Ergonomic JSON-document semantics; multi-language (Rust, JS, Swift, Java, Python); `automerge-repo` is the recommended high-level wrapper, ships network adapters including WebRTC. | Slightly larger payloads than Yjs in pure-text scenarios. |\n\nFor Meta Sovereign:\n\n- **Yjs** for the chat-text auto-completion buffer and any free-text editing surfaces.\n- **Automerge** (via `automerge-repo`) for structured records — contact, group, fact, dialog graph nodes.\n\nReferences:\n\n- Yjs — <https://yjs.dev/>\n- Automerge — <https://automerge.org/>\n- Velt comparison — <https://velt.dev/blog/best-crdt-libraries-real-time-data-sync>\n\n## 3. Personal CRM / Personal Knowledge Graph\n\n[**Monica**](https://www.monicahq.com/) (docs: <https://docs.monicahq.com/>) is the leading open-source PRM (Laravel-based, self-hostable). Useful as a **schema reference** for the Sovereign import layer — it already models people, activities, reminders, debts, gifts, conversations.\n\nFor graph-style PKG, see Logseq / Athens-style projects. The Sovereign data model is *more* than a PKG — it's a CRM + PKG hybrid — but the PKG community's experience around block-level addressing is informative.\n\n## 4. Unified messaging — legality and architecture\n\nBuilding \"one client for every messenger\" is a deeply ToS-sensitive area. The cleanest path is **Matrix bridges**:\n\n- **Beeper bridges** (developer docs: <https://developers.beeper.com/bridges>) bridge WhatsApp, Telegram, Signal, X, LinkedIn, etc. into a single Matrix homeserver. Bridges are open source under [`mautrix`](https://github.com/mautrix) and the `bridge-manager`.\n- **Element** (<https://element.io/>) is the reference Matrix client.\n- For Meta Sovereign: a self-hosted Matrix homeserver + mautrix bridges is a viable backend that keeps the user's data **on their machine** and lets the Sovereign UI consume the Matrix Client-Server API directly.\n\nThis does not preclude direct importers (VK takeout, Telegram takeout, etc.) — those remain useful for archival and for networks without good Matrix bridges.\n\n## 5. Pattern editor (regex / PEG inference)\n\nThere is no off-the-shelf interactive component for **example-driven regex/PEG inference**. Useful research baselines:\n\n- **REGAE** — Glassman Lab, UIST'20. Interactive regex synthesis with augmented examples and corner-case generation. <https://glassmanlab.seas.harvard.edu/papers/ips_augex_uist20.pdf>\n- **Sketch-Driven Regex Generation** — Chen et al., TACL. Combines NL + examples. <https://direct.mit.edu/tacl/article/doi/10.1162/tacl_a_00339/96479/Sketch-Driven-Regular-Expression-Generation-from>\n- **SplitRegex** — Neuro-symbolic regex synthesis. <https://arxiv.org/pdf/2205.11258>\n- **Ohm-JS** — production PEG playground. Best fit for the PEG side of R-C1. <https://ohmjs.org/>\n\nRealistic plan: build a pattern editor on top of Ohm-JS for PEGs and a small example-driven heuristic generator for regexes (escape literal text, infer character classes from the union of seen characters at each position). Defer real synthesis to a later milestone.\n\n## 6. Fuzzy search in JavaScript\n\n| Library | Strengths |\n| ------- | --------- |\n| **Fuse.js** | Bitap-based, zero deps, ideal for small/medium personal datasets and reply-template search. <https://www.fusejs.io/> |\n| **MiniSearch** | Inverted-index full-text with prefix/phrase support. <https://lucaong.github.io/minisearch/> |\n| **uFuzzy** | Faster on large lists. <https://github.com/leeoniya/uFuzzy> |\n\nPlan: hybrid stack — **MiniSearch** for tokenised full-text search across the whole message archive, **Fuse.js** for reply-variation fuzzy lookup (small lists, relevance scoring matters more than throughput).\n\n## 7. Node editors in JS (n8n-like)\n\n| Library | Why |\n| ------- | --- |\n| **Rete.js v2** | Ships with a real dataflow / control-flow execution engine, not just visual canvas. Closest match to n8n's runtime model. <https://retejs.org/> |\n| React Flow | Most-adopted React-only library for visual canvas. Excellent rendering, no execution engine. <https://reactflow.dev/> |\n| Drawflow | Minimal vanilla-JS option. <https://jerosoler.github.io/Drawflow/> |\n\nFor R-C3 (automated dialog graph), **Rete.js** wins: the dialog automation needs to actually *execute*, and Rete provides the engine for it.\n\n## 8. WebRTC peer-to-peer sync\n\nStack options:\n\n- **PeerJS** — wraps WebRTC signaling with a clean API for data channels. <https://peerjs.com/>\n- **simple-peer** — lower-level building block. <https://github.com/feross/simple-peer>\n- **automerge-repo** — ships a WebRTC network adapter alongside CRDT semantics. Recommended for CRDT sync scenarios. <https://automerge.org/docs/repositories/>\n- **RxDB WebRTC plugin** — DB-level replication. <https://rxdb.info/replication-webrtc.html>\n- **GunDB** — graph DB with built-in P2P. <https://gun.eco/>\n\nPlan: `automerge-repo` + WebRTC adapter for structured records, `y-webrtc` for collaborative text channels. PeerJS is a fallback if we need a pure data-channel transport.\n\n## 9. Messenger export formats and APIs\n\nPer-network legal export options (December 2025 / January 2026):\n\n| Network | Export route |\n| ------- | ------------ |\n| Telegram | Official **Takeout API** — JSON/HTML. <https://core.telegram.org/api/takeout> Telegram Desktop also exports JSON locally. |\n| WhatsApp | **Per-chat \"Export chat\"** (txt + media zip). WhatsApp Business Cloud API for opted-in flows; bulk export is restricted. |\n| Signal | Server-side export forbidden by design. **On-device backups** only — Android encrypted backup, Desktop's `signal-backup-decode`. |\n| Facebook | **Download Your Information** (HTML/JSON archive). |\n| X | Official **archive download** (JSON + media). |\n| LinkedIn | Official **Get a copy of your data** (CSV bundle). |\n| VK | HTML archive (parsed by `konard/vk-export`); native API. |\n| career.habr.com / hh.ru / superjob.ru | Public APIs and account exports — to be researched per network in follow-up. |\n\n[**MasterScrat/Chatistics**](https://github.com/MasterScrat/Chatistics) parses Messenger/Hangouts/WhatsApp/Telegram exports into DataFrames — useful import baseline.\n\n## 10. Encrypted-at-rest in Electron / browser\n\n| Tool | Where it fits |\n| ---- | ------------- |\n| **SQLCipher** | Transparent AES-256-CBC + PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA512 over SQLite. Works with `node-sqlite3` / `better-sqlite3` in Electron. <https://www.zetetic.net/sqlcipher/design/> |\n| **libsodium-wrappers** | Symmetric/asymmetric crypto in browser/Node. Small surface. <https://github.com/jedisct1/libsodium.js> |\n| **age** | File-level export bundles and key wrapping. <https://age-encryption.org/> |\n\nPlan: SQLCipher for the Doublets sidecar SQLite (if used) and any Electron cache; libsodium for envelope encryption of `.lino` exports; age for inter-machine bundle wrapping.\n\n## 11. SQLite / LevelDB in the browser (WASM)\n\n| Option | Notes |\n| ------ | ----- |\n| **Official `sqlite3.wasm`** | Canonical OPFS-backed build. Requires SharedArrayBuffer + COOP/COEP. <https://sqlite.org/wasm> |\n| **wa-sqlite** | Multiple VFS variants (OPFSAdaptive, IDBBatchAtomic). Works without COOP/COEP via Asyncify. <https://github.com/rhashimoto/wa-sqlite> |\n| **sql.js** | In-memory only — fine for read-only embedded data. <https://sql.js.org/> |\n| **absurd-sql** | Considered legacy. |\n| **level-js over IndexedDB** | Standard LevelDB-in-browser route. <https://github.com/Level/level-js> |\n\nFor Meta Sovereign's browser deployment, **sqlite3.wasm + OPFS** is the default; **wa-sqlite** is the fallback when COOP/COEP can't be configured (e.g. GitHub Pages).\n\nThe **primary** local-first store is still `doublets-web` (Doublets in WASM). SQLite-in-browser is a *secondary* option for ancillary search indexes (FTS5) where a relational index is more convenient than a Doublets query.\n\n## 12. Cross-platform shells (Electron + browser + mobile)\n\n| Option | Notes |\n| ------ | ----- |\n| **Tauri 2** | System webview, ~3-10 MB bundles, targets iOS/Android in v2. Strong fit for a Rust-heavy project. <https://v2.tauri.app/> |\n| **Electron** | Easiest for pure-JS teams; Chromium parity across OSes. <https://www.electronjs.org/> |\n| **Capacitor** | Web → mobile + Electron via plugin. Same web build to browser/mobile/desktop with a native shell. <https://capacitorjs.com/> |\n\nThe issue prescribes `deep-foundation/sdk` (R-G3). If `deep-foundation/sdk` proves insufficient for iOS/Android, the documented fallback is Tauri 2 (because of the existing Rust investment in `doublets-rs`) or Capacitor (if we want a single web-build everywhere).\n\n---\n\n## Index of cited URLs\n\n- Local-first essay — <https://www.inkandswitch.com/essay/local-first/>\n- Local-first talk — <https://martin.kleppmann.com/2019/10/23/local-first-at-onward.html>\n- Yjs — <https://yjs.dev/>\n- Automerge — <https://automerge.org/>\n- Velt CRDT comparison — <https://velt.dev/blog/best-crdt-libraries-real-time-data-sync>\n- Monica CRM — <https://www.monicahq.com/>\n- Monica docs — <https://docs.monicahq.com/>\n- Beeper bridges — <https://developers.beeper.com/bridges>\n- Beeper open source — <https://developers.beeper.com/open-source>\n- Element — <https://element.io/>\n- mautrix bridges — <https://github.com/mautrix>\n- REGAE / UIST'20 — <https://glassmanlab.seas.harvard.edu/papers/ips_augex_uist20.pdf>\n- Sketch-Driven Regex / TACL — <https://direct.mit.edu/tacl/article/doi/10.1162/tacl_a_00339/96479/Sketch-Driven-Regular-Expression-Generation-from>\n- SplitRegex — <https://arxiv.org/pdf/2205.11258>\n- Ohm-JS — <https://ohmjs.org/>\n- Fuse.js — <https://www.fusejs.io/>\n- MiniSearch — <https://lucaong.github.io/minisearch/>\n- React Flow — <https://reactflow.dev/>\n- Rete.js — <https://retejs.org/>\n- PeerJS — <https://peerjs.com/>\n- automerge-repo — <https://automerge.org/docs/repositories/>\n- RxDB WebRTC plugin — <https://rxdb.info/replication-webrtc.html>\n- GunDB — <https://gun.eco/>\n- Telegram Takeout API — <https://core.telegram.org/api/takeout>\n- SQLCipher design — <https://www.zetetic.net/sqlcipher/design/>\n- libsodium.js — <https://github.com/jedisct1/libsodium.js>\n- age — <https://age-encryption.org/>\n- SQLite WASM — <https://sqlite.org/wasm>\n- wa-sqlite — <https://github.com/rhashimoto/wa-sqlite>\n- sql.js — <https://sql.js.org/>\n- level-js — <https://github.com/Level/level-js>\n- Tauri 2 — <https://v2.tauri.app/>\n- Electron — <https://www.electronjs.org/>\n- Capacitor — <https://capacitorjs.com/>\n"

PeerJS is a fallback if we need a pure data-channel transport.\n\n## 9. Messenger export formats and APIs\n\nPer-network legal export options (December 2025 / January 2026):\n\n| Network | Export route |\n| ------- | ------------ |\n| Telegram | Official **Takeout API** — JSON/HTML. <https://core.telegram.org/api/takeout> Telegram Desktop also exports JSON locally. |\n| WhatsApp | **Per-chat \"Export chat\"** (txt + media zip). WhatsApp Business Cloud API for opted-in flows; bulk export is restricted. |\n| Signal | Server-side export forbidden by design. **On-device backups** only — Android encrypted backup, Desktop's `signal-backup-decode`. |\n| Facebook | **Download Your Information** (HTML/JSON archive). |\n| X | Official **archive download** (JSON + media). |\n| LinkedIn | Official **Get a copy of your data** (CSV bundle). |\n| VK | HTML archive (parsed by `konard/vk-export`); native API. |\n| career.habr.com / hh.ru / superjob.ru | Public APIs and account exports — to be researched per network in follow-up. |\n\n[**MasterScrat/Chatistics**](https://github.com/MasterScrat/Chatistics) parses Messenger/Hangouts/WhatsApp/Telegram exports into DataFrames — useful import baseline.\n\n## 10. Encrypted-at-rest in Electron / browser\n\n| Tool | Where it fits |\n| ---- | ------------- |\n| **SQLCipher** | Transparent AES-256-CBC + PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA512 over SQLite. Works with `node-sqlite3` / `better-sqlite3` in Electron. <https://www.zetetic.net/sqlcipher/design/> |\n| **libsodium-wrappers** | Symmetric/asymmetric crypto in browser/Node. Small surface. <https://github.com/jedisct1/libsodium.js> |\n| **age** | File-level export bundles and key wrapping. <https://age-encryption.org/> |\n\nPlan: SQLCipher for the Doublets sidecar SQLite (if used) and any Electron cache; libsodium for envelope encryption of `.lino` exports; age for inter-machine bundle wrapping.\n\n## 11. SQLite / LevelDB in the browser (WASM)\n\n| Option | Notes |\n| ------ | ----- |\n| **Official `sqlite3.wasm`** | Canonical OPFS-backed build. Requires SharedArrayBuffer + COOP/COEP. <https://sqlite.org/wasm> |\n| **wa-sqlite** | Multiple VFS variants (OPFSAdaptive, IDBBatchAtomic). Works without COOP/COEP via Asyncify. <https://github.com/rhashimoto/wa-sqlite> |\n| **sql.js** | In-memory only — fine for read-only embedded data. <https://sql.js.org/> |\n| **absurd-sql** | Considered legacy. |\n| **level-js over IndexedDB** | Standard LevelDB-in-browser route. <https://github.com/Level/level-js> |\n\nFor Meta Sovereign's browser deployment, **sqlite3.wasm + OPFS** is the default; **wa-sqlite** is the fallback when COOP/COEP can't be configured (e.g. GitHub Pages).\n\nThe **primary** local-first store is still `doublets-web` (Doublets in WASM). SQLite-in-browser is a *secondary* option for ancillary search indexes (FTS5) where a relational index is more convenient than a Doublets query.\n\n## 12. Cross-platform shells (Electron + browser + mobile)\n\n| Option | Notes |\n| ------ | ----- |\n| **Tauri 2** | System webview, ~3-10 MB bundles, targets iOS/Android in v2. Strong fit for a Rust-heavy project. <https://v2.tauri.app/> |\n| **Electron** | Easiest for pure-JS teams; Chromium parity across OSes. <https://www.electronjs.org/> |\n| **Capacitor** | Web → mobile + Electron via plugin. Same web build to browser/mobile/desktop with a native shell. <https://capacitorjs.com/> |\n\nThe issue prescribes `deep-foundation/sdk` (R-G3). If `deep-foundation/sdk` proves insufficient for iOS/Android, the documented fallback is Tauri 2 (because of the existing Rust investment in `doublets-rs`) or Capacitor (if we want a single web-build everywhere).\n\n---\n\n## Index of cited URLs\n\n- Local-first essay — <https://www.inkandswitch.com/essay/local-first/>\n- Local-first talk — <https://martin.kleppmann.com/2019/10/23/local-first-at-onward.html>\n- Yjs — <https://yjs.dev/>\n- Automerge — <https://automerge.org/>\n- Velt CRDT comparison — <https://velt.dev/blog/best-crdt-libraries-real-time-data-sync>\n- Monica CRM — <https://www.monicahq.com/>\n- Monica docs — <https://docs.monicahq.com/>\n- Beeper bridges — <https://developers.beeper.com/bridges>\n- Beeper open source — <https://developers.beeper.com/open-source>\n- Element — <https://element.io/>\n- mautrix bridges — <https://github.com/mautrix>\n- REGAE / UIST'20 — <https://glassmanlab.seas.harvard.edu/papers/ips_augex_uist20.pdf>\n- Sketch-Driven Regex / TACL — [2026-05-01T08:52:02.586Z] [INFO]           "content": "# Architecture — Meta Sovereign 0.0.1\n\nThis document sketches the high-level architecture of the prototype. It satisfies R-A3 (dual binary+text store), R-F8 (universal Links access in client and server), R-G1 (JS + Rust/WASM stack) and R-G2 (pure-Rust stack option). It is intentionally lightweight — the aim is to lock in interfaces, not pre-design every module.\n\n## 1. Layered overview\n\n```\n              +------------------------------------------------+\n              |                  UI layer                       |\n              |  Operator UI  |  Unified Chat UI  |  CRM UI    |\n              |  Pattern Editor | Reply Editor | Dialog Graph   |\n              +------------------------------------------------+\n                                   |\n              +------------------------------------------------+\n              |          Universal Links Access API            |\n              |    read/write to Doublets (binary) AND Lino    |\n              |          (text), client-side and server-side   |\n              +------------------------------------------------+\n                                   |\n   +-------------------+   +---------------------+   +---------------+\n   |    Importers      |   |   CRDT Sync layer   |   |  Automation   |\n   |  (per network)    |   |  Automerge / Yjs    |   |  Engine       |\n   |  VK / TG / WA /   |   |  WebRTC adapter     |   |  Rete.js v2   |\n   |  FB / X / LI /    |   +---------------------+   |  patterns/    |\n   |  hh.ru / habr /   |                              |  replies      |\n   |  superjob.ru      |                              +---------------+\n   +-------------------+\n                                   |\n              +------------------------------------------------+\n              |               Storage layer                     |\n              |  Doublets (doublets-rs / doublets-web WASM)    |\n              |  Links Notation (links-notation, lino-codec)   |\n              |  Optional FTS index (sqlite-wasm + OPFS)        |\n              |  Encrypted-at-rest envelope (libsodium / age)  |\n              +------------------------------------------------+\n                                   |\n              +------------------------------------------------+\n              |               Runtime shell                     |\n              |  CLI (lino-arguments)                          |\n              |  Local web server (Node/Bun)                   |\n              |  Electron app (deep-foundation/sdk)            |\n              |  Browser PWA  (sqlite-wasm + doublets-web)     |\n              |  Optional Docker microservices: web + WebRTC   |\n              +------------------------------------------------+\n```\n\n## 2. Universal Links Access API\n\nA single TypeScript interface (also exposed in Rust) with the **same signature** on client and server:\n\n```ts\ninterface UniversalLinksAccess {\n  // Reads (binary store first, falls back to text store)\n  query(pattern: LinkPattern): AsyncIterable<Link>;\n  get(id: LinkId): Promise<Link | null>;\n\n  // Writes (write-through to both stores)\n  put(link: Link): Promise<LinkId>;\n  delete(id: LinkId): Promise<void>;\n\n  // Sync hooks (used by the CRDT sync layer)\n  subscribe(filter: LinkPattern, cb: (event: LinkEvent) => void): Unsubscribe;\n}\n```\n\n- On the **server**, both stores are local files; the implementation writes to Doublets (binary) and to `.lino` text files at the same time. Backups (R-A4) are atomic snapshots of both.\n- On the **browser/Electron client**, the implementation backs onto `doublets-web` (WASM) for the binary store and onto an in-memory Lino representation that is periodically flushed to OPFS / the user's filesystem.\n- The CRDT sync layer never talks to the disk directly — it always goes through this API.\n\n## 3. Storage layer\n\n| Concern | Choice | Rationale |\n| ------- | ------ | --------- |\n| Primary binary store | `doublets-rs` (server) / `doublets-web` (client) | Issue-prescribed; fast, mmap, language-neutral. |\n| Primary text store | `links-notation` + `lino-objects-codec` | Issue-prescribed; portable, diffable, human-readable. |\n| Search index | `sqlite-wasm` + FTS5 (browser, OPFS) / SQLCipher (Electron) | Optional — kept as a sidecar derived from the primary stores; can be regenerated. |\n| Encryption | `libsodium` (envelope on `.lino` exports) and `age` (cross-machine bundles) | The primary stores are unencrypted on disk; users opt into encryption per export. SQLCipher protects the search index at rest in Electron. |\n| Backup format | `.tar.zst` containing `data.lino`, `doublets.bin`, `meta.json` | Self-describing, restorable on any platform. |\n\n## 4. Importer adapters\n\nEvery network gets the same shape:\n\n```ts\ninterface MessageSource<Cursor = unknown> {\n  readonly id: 'vk' | 'telegram' | 'x' | 'whatsapp' | 'facebook'\n             | 'linkedin' | 'habr' | 'hh' | 'superjob';\n  init(opts: AdapterOpts): Promise<void>;\n  pull(since?: Cursor): AsyncIterable<RawMessage>;\n  importArchive(path: string): AsyncIterable<RawMessage>;\n}\n```\n\nEach adapter is a separate NPM package (R-F1) and depends on the existing konard tools (`vk-export`, `telegram-bot`, `broadcast`, etc.) for the heavy lifting. The adapters convert their network-specific payloads into a uniform `Message` link shape that the storage layer ingests.\n\n## 5. CRDT sync layer\n\n- `automerge-repo` for structured records (contacts, groups, facts, dialog graph).\n- `Yjs` (with `y-webrtc`) for collaborative free-text fields (reply variations, draft messages).\n- Each user-owned device participates as a peer; one device may optionally act as the user's personal cloud relay (R-F5, R-F6).\n- The sync layer subscribes to `UniversalLinksAccess.subscribe` to translate local writes into CRDT operations.\n\n## 6. Automation engine\n\n- **Rete.js v2** owns the runtime graph: nodes are patterns, branches, reply-variation pickers, and side-effect actions (send to network).\n- Nodes are persisted as Doublets links (R-A3), so the dialog graph itself is part of the unified database — backed up, exported, synced like everything else.\n- Two execution modes (R-C4):\n  - *Auto:* the engine fires on every match and picks a reply variation.\n  - *Semi-auto:* the engine surfaces a candidate reply in the Operator UI; user confirms, edits, or rejects.\n\n## 7. UI surfaces\n\n| Surface | Purpose | Stack |\n| ------- | ------- | ----- |\n| **Unified Chat UI** | One inbox spanning all networks. | React + Tauri/Electron + WebRTC live updates. |\n| **Operator UI** | Card-stream interface (DONE/NEXT) over unread items. | React; mirrors `link-assistant/operator`. |\n| **CRM UI** | Contact/group/community pages, intersections, mass-personal outreach. | React + table virtualisation + saved-query model. |\n| **Pattern Editor** | Build regex/PEG patterns from example messages. | React + Ohm-JS playground; hot-loads candidates from MiniSearch. |\n| **Reply-Variation Editor** | Manage groups of variant replies. | React; Fuse.js for fuzzy lookup against history. |\n| **Dialog Graph** | n8n-like canvas of patterns + replies. | React + Rete.js v2. |\n| **Backup/Settings** | Profile sync, resume sync, encryption keys, backup target folder. | React. |\n\n## 8. Runtime shells (R-F2 / R-F3 / R-F4)\n\n| Shell | Description |\n| ----- | ----------- |\n| `meta-sovereign` CLI | Subcommands for import, export, backup, key management, sync; built on `lino-arguments`. |\n| `meta-sovereign-web` | Local web server (Node or Bun) that exposes the Universal Links API over HTTP/WebSocket. Used by the browser PWA when run on the same machine. |\n| `meta-sovereign-desktop` | Electron app (Tauri optional) wrapping the same React bundle. |\n| Docker images | `meta-sovereign-web` and `meta-sovereign-rtc` (WebRTC signalling + relay) for personal-cloud deployment. |\n\n## 9. Pure-Rust parity track (R-G2)\n\nA second runtime is planned in pure Rust:\n\n- `meta-sovereign-rs/server` — equivalent of `meta-sovereign-web`, using `axum` + `doublets-rs` + a Rust port of `lino-objects-codec`.\n- `meta-sovereign-rs/cli` — equivalent of the CLI; uses `clap` + `lino-arguments` Rust bindings.\n- The on-disk format (Doublets + `.lino`) is shared; users can switch stacks without re-importing.\n\n## 10. Cross-cutting concerns\n\n- **Backups (R-A4)**: a daemon job (cron in CLI / Electron timer / Docker sidecar) snapshots the primary stores to `${backup_dir}/YYYY/MM/DD/HH-MM-SS.tar.zst`. Retention rules are configured in `lino-arguments`.\n- **Logging**: structured logs to local file; never network-ingressed by default.\n- **Telemetry**: opt-in only; off by default. The whole architecture stays usable with telemetry permanently disabled.\n- **Testing (R-H4)**: unit tests per package, integration tests per adapter (against fixture archives), e2e tests through `browser-commander` against the Electron app.\n- **Docs (R-H3)**: TypeDoc for JS/TS packages, rustdoc for Rust crates; aggregated to a top-level `docs/api/` site.\n- **CI parity (R-H5)**: every package inherits the JS pipeline template; every Rust crate inherits the Rust pipeline template. Discrepancies discovered during integration are filed as issues against the templates.\n\n## 11. Out of scope for v0.0.1\n\n- No federated identity / no multi-user accounts. The whole system assumes a single user.\n- No automated content moderation / NSFW filtering. The user owns the policy.\n- No on-device LLM. Out of scope, can be added later as an automation node type.\n- No paid API integrations. All connectors must work with free-tier APIs or local exports.\n"

Cross-cutting concerns\n\n- **Backups (R-A4)**: a daemon job (cron in CLI / Electron timer / Docker sidecar) snapshots the primary stores to `${backup_dir}/YYYY/MM/DD/HH-MM-SS.tar.zst`. Retention rules are configured in `lino-arguments`.\n- **Logging**: structured logs to local file; never network-ingressed by default.\n- **Telemetry**: opt-in only; off by default. The whole architecture stays usable with telemetry permanently disabled.\n- **Testing (R-H4)**: unit tests per package, integration tests per adapter (against fixture archives), e2e tests through `browser-commander` against the Electron app.\n- **Docs (R-H3)**: TypeDoc for JS/TS packages, rustdoc for Rust crates; aggregated to a top-level `docs/api/` site.\n- **CI parity (R-H5)**: every package inherits the JS pipeline template; every Rust crate inherits the Rust pipeline template. Discrepancies discovered during integration are filed as issues against the templates.\n\n## 11. Out of scope for v0.0.1\n\n- No federated identity / no multi-user accounts. The whole system assumes a single user.\n- No automated content moderation / NSFW filtering. The user owns the policy.\n- No on-device LLM. Out of scope, can be added later as an automation node type.\n- No paid API integrations. All connectors must work with free-tier APIs or local exports.\n",

[2026-05-01T08:53:09.415Z] [INFO]           "content": "# Solution Plan — Issue #1\n\nThis plan groups the requirements in `requirements.md` into milestones. Each milestone lists the requirement IDs it satisfies, the deliverables, and the tools / libraries used. The plan is **incremental** — every milestone leaves the project in a runnable state.\n\nThe plan respects the issue's non-negotiables: simple code first (R-H2), no premature optimisation, every feature an importable NPM package (R-F1), public-domain license (R-H6), unit + integration + e2e tests on every milestone (R-H4).\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 0 — Case study & repository identity (this PR)\n\n| Requirement | Deliverable |\n| ----------- | ----------- |\n| R-I1 | `docs/case-studies/issue-1/` folder with this plan, requirements list, components catalogue, external research, architecture sketch. |\n| R-I2 | `external-research.md` with public sources cited. |\n| R-I3 | `requirements.md` with stable IDs. |\n| R-I4 | `solution-plan.md` (this file). |\n| R-I5 | `components.md` cataloguing every issue-linked repository plus relevant external libraries. |\n| Repository identity | `package.json` `name` / `description`, README header reflecting Meta Sovereign instead of the template name. |\n\n**Status**: in progress in PR #2.\n\n**Out of scope**: any feature implementation. Everything below is a follow-up PR.\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 1 — Storage skeleton (Universal Links Access)\n\n**Requirements:** R-A2, R-A3, R-A5, R-F8 (server side), R-G1.\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. NPM package `@meta-sovereign/storage` exposing `UniversalLinksAccess` (`get`, `put`, `delete`, `query`, `subscribe`).\n2. Two implementations behind the same interface:\n   - `LinoTextStore` — backed by `links-notation` + `lino-objects-codec`, persists to a configurable directory.\n   - `DoubletsStore` — backed by `doublets-web` (WASM) for browser/Electron, backed by an N-API binding to `doublets-rs` on Node/Bun (or shelling out to `link-cli` as a fallback).\n3. A `DualStore` that wraps both and write-throughs every mutation, with an integrity check that compares the two on demand.\n4. Test fixtures: a small `.lino` file that round-trips through the codec without loss.\n5. Examples in `examples/` showing how to import/export a tiny graph.\n\n**Risks:**\n\n- WASM bundling for `doublets-web` in Electron renderer (CSP). Mitigation: bundle as Node-side service, expose over IPC.\n- Performance of the dual write. Mitigation: queue text writes and flush in batches.\n\n**Tests:**\n\n- Unit: codec round-trip.\n- Integration: dual write, query consistency, on-disk recovery after kill -9.\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 2 — Backups & `.lino` import/export\n\n**Requirements:** R-A1, R-A2, R-A4.\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. `meta-sovereign backup` CLI subcommand that snapshots the storage directory to `${backup_dir}/YYYY/MM/DD/HH-MM-SS.tar.zst`.\n2. Retention policy configurable via `lino-arguments` (keep N daily, M weekly, etc.).\n3. `meta-sovereign import --file=foo.lino` and `meta-sovereign export --file=bar.lino`.\n4. e2e test: import → mutate → export → diff yields a recognisable change set.\n\n**Tools:** `lino-arguments`, `tar`, `zstd-codec` or `node:zlib`/system `zstd`.\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 3 — First importer adapter (Telegram)\n\n**Requirements:** R-E2, R-A1, partial R-D1.\n\nTelegram is the first adapter because the konard repos already provide a working implementation (`telegram-bot`, `follow`, `telegramify-markdown`).\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. NPM package `@meta-sovereign/source-telegram` implementing `MessageSource`.\n2. `meta-sovereign import --source=telegram --archive=path` for Telegram Desktop JSON exports.\n3. `meta-sovereign import --source=telegram --takeout` for the live Takeout API.\n4. Mapping from Telegram message → unified `Message` link, including: sender, chat, timestamp, reply-to, edits, media references.\n5. Integration tests against fixture archives.\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 4 — Second importer (VK), establishes the adapter pattern\n\n**Requirements:** R-E1, R-A1.\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. `@meta-sovereign/source-vk` wrapping `vk-export` (HTML archive) and the Kate-Mobile token pipeline (`vk-bot` / `vk-browser`).\n2. The `MessageSource` interface is finalised based on lessons from Telegram + VK; documented in `architecture.md` as stable.\n3. Tests against real archive samples (anonymised).\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 5 — Unified Chat UI (read-only)\n\n**Requirements:** R-B1.\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. NPM package `@meta-sovereign/web` — a React + Vite app that reads from `UniversalLinksAccess` over HTTP/WebSocket.\n2. Chat list, message list with virtualised scrolling, search bar (powered by MiniSearch).\n3. UI quality bench-marked against Telegram Desktop.\n4. e2e tests via `browser-commander`.\n\n**Out of scope at this milestone:** sending messages back, auto-completion, operator mode, multi-network differences (each network is rendered the same way).\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 6 — Operator UI\n\n**Requirements:** R-B3, partial R-B2.\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. Card-stream view modeled on `link-assistant/operator`. Two actions: DONE / NEXT.\n2. Auto-completion in the message composer fed by MiniSearch over the user's outgoing messages.\n3. Keyboard-driven workflow (D / N for DONE/NEXT, ⌘↵ to send, etc.).\n4. e2e tests for the queue progression.\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 7 — Pattern editor + reply-variation editor\n\n**Requirements:** R-C1, R-C2, R-C5.\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. NPM package `@meta-sovereign/patterns` with a `Pattern` model (regex or PEG via Ohm-JS), simplification heuristics, and an example-driven generator.\n2. NPM package `@meta-sovereign/replies` with a `ReplyVariationGroup` model and a Fuse.js-based fuzzy candidate finder.\n3. UI: pattern editor (left: examples, right: live regex/PEG, bottom: matches), reply-variation editor (group list with tag chips).\n4. Fact extractor that, given a patterns set, scans conversations for `question → answer` pairs (R-C5). Output rendered per participant in group chats.\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 8 — Dialog automation graph\n\n**Requirements:** R-C3, R-C4.\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. Rete.js v2 canvas integrated into `@meta-sovereign/web`.\n2. Node types: `PatternNode`, `BranchNode`, `ReplyVariationNode`, `SendMessageNode`, `WaitForNode`.\n3. Two run modes (auto / semi-auto). Semi-auto surfaces candidate replies in the Operator UI.\n4. The graph is persisted via `UniversalLinksAccess` so it is part of the unified database.\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 9 — CRM features\n\n**Requirements:** R-D1, R-D2, R-D3, R-D4, partial R-D5/R-D6.\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. Contact-detail page that aggregates everything known (chats, groups, communities, extracted facts).\n2. Saved-query language for set intersections (e.g. `group(\"Foo\") AND fact(\"speaks_russian\")`).\n3. Mass-personal kick-off helper (templated greeting per contact in a saved query).\n4. Local search (R-D4) wired to MiniSearch + Fuse.js.\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 10 — Sync layer (WebRTC + CRDT)\n\n**Requirements:** R-F5, R-F6.\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. `automerge-repo` integrated against `UniversalLinksAccess`.\n2. WebRTC adapter (`@automerge/automerge-repo-network-webrtc`).\n3. Optional Docker microservice `meta-sovereign-rtc` that runs the signalling server (R-F6).\n4. End-to-end test: two browsers connect, mutate, see each other's changes.\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 11 — Outbound broadcasting & profile sync\n\n**Requirements:** R-B4, R-D5, R-D6.\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. `@meta-sovereign/broadcast` integrating `konard/broadcast` for X / Telegram / VK posting.\n2. Profile sync UI that pushes the user's avatar / bio to every authenticated network (gracefully no-ops where APIs forbid it).\n3. Resume sync for hh.ru, career.habr.com, superjob.ru, LinkedIn (R-E6, R-E7, R-E8, R-E9).\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 12 — Remaining importers\n\n**Requirements:** R-E3, R-E4, R-E5, R-E6, R-E7, R-E8, R-E9.\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. `@meta-sovereign/source-x` (X archive importer).\n2. `@meta-sovereign/source-whatsapp` (per-chat export importer).\n3. `@meta-sovereign/source-facebook` (download-your-data archive).\n4. `@meta-sovereign/source-linkedin` (data export archive).\n5. `@meta-sovereign/source-habr-career`, `@meta-sovereign/source-hh`, `@meta-sovereign/source-superjob` (resume + applications sync).\n\nEach follows the `MessageSource` interface finalised in milestone 4.\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 13 — Pure-Rust stack\n\n**Requirements:** R-G2.\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. `meta-sovereign-rs/server` (`axum` + `doublets-rs` + Rust lino codec).\n2. `meta-sovereign-rs/cli`.\n3. Conformance test suite that runs the same fixture imports through both stacks and diffs the resulting Doublets file.\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 14 — Mobile and Electron polish\n\n**Requirements:** R-F3, R-G3, R-H1.\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. Electron app via `deep-foundation/sdk`, with auto-update.\n2. Mobile builds via Capacitor (or Tauri 2 if `deep-foundation/sdk` is not yet mobile-ready).\n3. HIG / Material / Microsoft design audit per surface.\n\n---\n\n## Cross-milestone concerns\n\n- **Each milestone ships a changeset** describing the new packages and any user-visible changes.\n- **Each milestone updates `requirements.md` traceability** — which IDs are now done, which are partial.\n- **CI gaps** (vs the JS template / Rust template) discovered during a milestone are filed as issues against the upstream template.\n- **Documentation site** is regenerated whenever an `@meta-sovereign/*` package version bumps.\n\n## Definition of done for v0.0.1\n\nThe smallest end-to-end demo that exercises every architectural layer is:\n\n> Import a Telegram archive, see the conversations in the Unified Chat UI, build a \"thank-you\" pattern + reply variation, run the dialog graph in semi-auto mode, accept the reply, see the reply broadcast back to the network, sync the new state to a second browser via WebRTC.\n\nReaching this end-to-end demo requires Milestones 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10, 11. Milestones 4, 9, 12, 13, 14 expand the system but are not on the critical path for the first demo.\n"

[2026-05-01T08:53:09.430Z] [INFO]     "content": "# Solution Plan — Issue #1\n\nThis plan groups the requirements in `requirements.md` into milestones. Each milestone lists the requirement IDs it satisfies, the deliverables, and the tools / libraries used. The plan is **incremental** — every milestone leaves the project in a runnable state.\n\nThe plan respects the issue's non-negotiables: simple code first (R-H2), no premature optimisation, every feature an importable NPM package (R-F1), public-domain license (R-H6), unit + integration + e2e tests on every milestone (R-H4).\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 0 — Case study & repository identity (this PR)\n\n| Requirement | Deliverable |\n| ----------- | ----------- |\n| R-I1 | `docs/case-studies/issue-1/` folder with this plan, requirements list, components catalogue, external research, architecture sketch. |\n| R-I2 | `external-research.md` with public sources cited. |\n| R-I3 | `requirements.md` with stable IDs. |\n| R-I4 | `solution-plan.md` (this file). |\n| R-I5 | `components.md` cataloguing every issue-linked repository plus relevant external libraries. |\n| Repository identity | `package.json` `name` / `description`, README header reflecting Meta Sovereign instead of the template name. |\n\n**Status**: in progress in PR #2.\n\n**Out of scope**: any feature implementation. Everything below is a follow-up PR.\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 1 — Storage skeleton (Universal Links Access)\n\n**Requirements:** R-A2, R-A3, R-A5, R-F8 (server side), R-G1.\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. NPM package `@meta-sovereign/storage` exposing `UniversalLinksAccess` (`get`, `put`, `delete`, `query`, `subscribe`).\n2. Two implementations behind the same interface:\n   - `LinoTextStore` — backed by `links-notation` + `lino-objects-codec`, persists to a configurable directory.\n   - `DoubletsStore` — backed by `doublets-web` (WASM) for browser/Electron, backed by an N-API binding to `doublets-rs` on Node/Bun (or shelling out to `link-cli` as a fallback).\n3. A `DualStore` that wraps both and write-throughs every mutation, with an integrity check that compares the two on demand.\n4. Test fixtures: a small `.lino` file that round-trips through the codec without loss.\n5. Examples in `examples/` showing how to import/export a tiny graph.\n\n**Risks:**\n\n- WASM bundling for `doublets-web` in Electron renderer (CSP). Mitigation: bundle as Node-side service, expose over IPC.\n- Performance of the dual write. Mitigation: queue text writes and flush in batches.\n\n**Tests:**\n\n- Unit: codec round-trip.\n- Integration: dual write, query consistency, on-disk recovery after kill -9.\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 2 — Backups & `.lino` import/export\n\n**Requirements:** R-A1, R-A2, R-A4.\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. `meta-sovereign backup` CLI subcommand that snapshots the storage directory to `${backup_dir}/YYYY/MM/DD/HH-MM-SS.tar.zst`.\n2. Retention policy configurable via `lino-arguments` (keep N daily, M weekly, etc.).\n3. `meta-sovereign import --file=foo.lino` and `meta-sovereign export --file=bar.lino`.\n4. e2e test: import → mutate → export → diff yields a recognisable change set.\n\n**Tools:** `lino-arguments`, `tar`, `zstd-codec` or `node:zlib`/system `zstd`.\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 3 — First importer adapter (Telegram)\n\n**Requirements:** R-E2, R-A1, partial R-D1.\n\nTelegram is the first adapter because the konard repos already provide a working implementation (`telegram-bot`, `follow`, `telegramify-markdown`).\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. NPM package `@meta-sovereign/source-telegram` implementing `MessageSource`.\n2. `meta-sovereign import --source=telegram --archive=path` for Telegram Desktop JSON exports.\n3. `meta-sovereign import --source=telegram --takeout` for the live Takeout API.\n4. Mapping from Telegram message → unified `Message` link, including: sender, chat, timestamp, reply-to, edits, media references.\n5. Integration tests against fixture archives.\n\n---\n\n## Milestone 4 — Second importer (VK), establishes the adapter pattern\n\n**Requirements:** R-E1, R-A1.\n\n**Deliverables:**\n\n1. `@meta-sovereign/source-vk` wrapping `vk-export` (HTML archive) and the Kate-Mobile token pipeline (`vk-bot` / `vk-browser`).\n2. Resolves two bugs in GitHub release formatting:\\n\\n1. **Removes incorrect section headers** (\\\"### Minor Changes\\\", \\\"### Major Changes\\\", \\\"### Patch Changes\\\") from release notes\\n2. **Enables PR detection** for all release types (Major, Minor, Patch)\\n\\n## Problem\\n\\nThe release v0.1.0 showed:\\n- ❌ Incorrect header \\\"### Minor Changes\\\" in release notes\\n- ❌ No link to the related pull request #56\\n\\n### Root Cause\\n\\nThe `scripts/format-release-notes.mjs` script only handled `### Patch Changes` sections:\\n\\n```javascript\\n// Old code - only matched Patch changes\\nconst patchChangesMatchWithHash = currentBody.match(\\n  /### Patch Changes\\\\s*\\\\n\\\\s*-\\\\s+([a-f0-9]+):\\\\s+(.+?)$/s\\n);\\n```\\n\\nWhen a Minor or Major release was created:\\n1. The regex pattern failed to match\\n2. The script exited early with \\\"⚠️ Could not parse patch changes\\\"\\n3. The section header remained in the release notes\\n4. PR detection was skipped entirely\\n\\n## Solution\\n\\nUpdated the regex to match **all** changeset types:\\n\\n```javascript\\n// New code - matches Major, Minor, and Patch changes\\nconst changesPattern =\\n  /### (Major|Minor|Patch) Changes\\\\s*\\\\n\\\\s*-\\\\s+(?:([a-f0-9]+):\\\\s+)?(.+?)$/s;\\n```\\n\\n### Changes Made\\n\\n**Modified Files:**\\n- `scripts/format-release-notes.mjs` - With commit hash: \"### [Major|Minor|Patch] Changes\\n- abc1234: Description\"\n  // 2. Without commit hash: \"### [Major|Minor|Patch] Changes\\n- Description\"\n  const changesPattern =\n    /### (Major|Minor|Patch) Changes\\s*\\n\\s*-\\s+(?:([a-f0-9]+):\\s+)?(.+?)$/s;\n  const changesMatch = currentBody.match(changesPattern);\n\n  let commitHash = null;\n  let rawDescription = null;\n  let changeType = null;\n\n  if (changesMatch) {\n    // Extract: [full match, changeType, commitHash (optional), description]\n    [, changeType, commitHash, rawDescription] = changesMatch;\n    console.log(`ℹ️ Found ${changeType} Changes section`);\n\n    // If commitHash is undefined and description contains it, try to extract\n    if (!commitHash && rawDescription) {\n      // This handles the case where description itself might be null/undefined\n      // and we need to safely check for commit hash at the start\n      const descWithHashMatch = rawDescription.match(/^([a-f0-9]+):\\s+(.+)$/s);\n      if (descWithHashMatch) {\n        [, commitHash, rawDescription] = descWithHashMatch;\n      }\n    }\n  } else {\n    console.log('⚠️ Could not parse changes from release notes');\n    console.log('   Looking for pattern: ### [Major|Minor|Patch] Changes');\n    process.exit(0);\n  }\n\n  // Clean up the description:\n  // 1. not adding PR link'\n        );\n      }\n    } catch (error) {\n      console.log('⚠️ Could not find PR for commit', commitShaToLookup);\n      console.log('   Error:', error.message);\n      if (process.env.DEBUG) {\n        console.error(error);\n      }\n    }\n  } else {\n    // No commit hash available from any source\n    console.log('ℹ️ No commit SHA available - not adding PR link');\n  }\n\n  // Build formatted release notes\n  const versionWithoutV = version.replace(/^v/, '');\n  const npmBadge = `[![npm version](https://img.shields.io/badge/npm-${versionWithoutV}-blue.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/${PACKAGE_NAME}/v/${versionWithoutV})`;\n\n  let formattedBody = `${cleanDescription}`;\n\n  // Add PR link if available\n  if (prNumber) {\n    formattedBody += `\\n\\n**Related Pull Request:** #${prNumber}`;\n  }\n\n  formattedBody += `\\n\\n---\\n\\n${npmBadge}`;\n\n  // Update the release using JSON input to properly handle special characters\n  const updatePayload = JSON.stringify({ body: formattedBody });\n  await $`gh api repos/${repository}/releases/${releaseId} -X PATCH --input -`.run(\n    { stdin: updatePayload }\n  );\n\n  console.log(`✅ Formatted release notes for v${versionWithoutV}`);\n  if (prNumber) {\n    console.log(`   - Added link to PR #${prNumber}`);\n  }\n  console.log('   - Added shields.io npm badge');\n  console.log('   - Cleaned up formatting');\n} catch (error) {\n  console.error('❌ Error formatting release notes:', error.message);\n  process.exit(1);\n}\n",

[2026-05-01T08:53:53.423Z] [INFO]     "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env bun\n\n/**\n * Merge multiple changeset files into a single changeset\n *\n * Key behavior:\n * - Combines all pending changesets into a single changeset file\n * - Uses the highest version bump type (major > minor > patch)\n * - Preserves all descriptions in chronological order (by file modification time)\n * - Removes the individual changeset files after merging\n * - Does nothing if there's only one or no changesets\n *\n * This script is run before `changeset version` to ensure a clean release\n * even when multiple PRs have merged before a release cycle.\n *\n * IMPORTANT: Update the package name below to match your package.json\n */\n\nimport {\n  readdirSync,\n  readFileSync,\n  writeFileSync,\n  unlinkSync,\n  statSync,\n} from 'fs';\nimport { join } from 'path';\n\n// TODO: Update this to match your package name in package.json\nconst PACKAGE_NAME = 'my-package';\nconst CHANGESET_DIR = '.changeset';\n\n// Version bump type priority (higher number = higher priority)\nconst BUMP_PRIORITY = {\n  patch: 1,\n  minor: 2,\n  major: 3,\n};\n\n/**\n * Generate a random changeset file name (similar to what @changesets/cli does)\n * @returns {string}\n */\nfunction generateChangesetName() {\n  const adjectives = [\n    'bright',\n    'calm',\n    'cool',\n    'cyan',\n    'dark',\n    'fast',\n    'gold',\n    'good',\n    'green',\n    'happy',\n    'kind',\n    'loud',\n    'neat',\n    'nice',\n    'pink',\n    'proud',\n    'quick',\n    'red',\n    'rich',\n    'safe',\n    'shy',\n    'soft',\n    'sweet',\n    'tall',\n    'warm',\n    'wise',\n    'young',\n  ];\n  const nouns = [\n    'apple',\n    'bird',\n    'book',\n    'car',\n    'cat',\n    'cloud',\n    'desk',\n    'dog',\n    'door',\n    'fish',\n    'flower',\n    'frog',\n    'grass',\n    'house',\n    'key',\n    'lake',\n    'leaf',\n    'moon',\n    'mouse',\n    'owl',\n    'park',\n    'rain',\n    'river',\n    'rock',\n    'sea',\n    'star',\n    'sun',\n    'tree',\n    'wave',\n    'wind',\n  ];\n\n  const randomAdjective =\n    adjectives[Math.floor(Math.random() * adjectives.length)];\n  const randomNoun = nouns[Math.floor(Math.random() * nouns.length)];\n\n  return `${randomAdjective}-${randomNoun}`;\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse a changeset file and extract its metadata\n * @param {string} filePath\n * @returns {{type: string, description: string, mtime: Date} | null}\n */\nfunction parseChangeset(filePath) {\n  try {\n    const content = readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8');\n    const stats = statSync(filePath);\n\n    // Extract version type - support both quoted and unquoted package names\n    const versionTypeRegex = new RegExp(\n      `^['\"]?${PACKAGE_NAME.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\\]\\\\]/g, '\\\\$&')}['\"]?:\\\\s+(major|minor|patch)`,\n      'm'\n    );\n    const versionTypeMatch = content.match(versionTypeRegex);\n\n    if (!versionTypeMatch) {\n      console.warn(\n        `Warning: Could not parse version type from ${filePath}, skipping`\n      );\n      return null;\n    }\n\n    // Extract description\n    const parts = content.split('---');\n    const description =\n      parts.length >= 3 ? parts.slice(2).join('---').trim() : '';\n\n    return {\n      type: versionTypeMatch[1],\n      description,\n      mtime: stats.mtime,\n    };\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.warn(`Warning: Failed to parse ${filePath}: ${error.message}`);\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Get the highest priority bump type\n * @param {string[]} types\n * @returns {string}\n */\nfunction getHighestBumpType(types) {\n  let highest = 'patch';\n  for (const type of types) {\n    if (BUMP_PRIORITY[type] > BUMP_PRIORITY[highest]) {\n      highest = type;\n    }\n  }\n  return highest;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a merged changeset file\n * @param {string} type\n * @param {string[]} descriptions\n * @returns {string}\n */\nfunction createMergedChangeset(type, descriptions) {\n  const combinedDescription = descriptions.join('\\n\\n');\n\n  return `---\n'${PACKAGE_NAME}': ${type}\n---\n\n${combinedDescription}\n`;\n}\n\nfunction main() {\n  console.log('Checking for multiple changesets to merge...');\n\n  // Get all changeset files\n  const changesetFiles = readdirSync(CHANGESET_DIR).filter(\n    (file) => file.endsWith('.md') && file !== 'README.md'\n  );\n\n  console.log(`Found ${changesetFiles.length} changeset file(s)`);\n\n  // If 0 or 1 changesets, nothing to merge\n  if (changesetFiles.length <= 1) {\n    console.log('No merging needed (0 or 1 changeset found)');\n    return;\n  }\n\n  console.log('Multiple changesets found, merging...');\n  changesetFiles.forEach((file) => console.log(`  - ${file}`));\n\n  // Parse all changesets\n  const parsedChangesets = [];\n  for (const file of changesetFiles) {\n    const filePath = join(CHANGESET_DIR, file);\n    const parsed = parseChangeset(filePath);\n    if (parsed) {\n      parsedChangesets.push({\n        file,\n        filePath,\n        ...parsed,\n      });\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (parsedChangesets.length === 0) {\n    console.error('Error: No valid changesets could be parsed');\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  // Sort by modification time (oldest first) to preserve chronological order\n  parsedChangesets.sort((a, b) => a.mtime.getTime() - b.mtime.getTime());\n\n  // Determine the highest bump type\n  const bumpTypes = parsedChangesets.map((c) => c.type);\n  const highestBumpType = getHighestBumpType(bumpTypes);\n\n  console.log(`\\nMerge summary:`);\n  console.log(`  Bump types found: ${[...new Set(bumpTypes)].join(', ')}`);\n  console.log(`  Using highest: ${highestBumpType}`);\n\n  // Collect descriptions in chronological order\n  const descriptions = parsedChangesets\n    .filter((c) => c.description)\n    .map((c) => c.description);\n\n  console.log(`  Descriptions to merge: ${descriptions.length}`);\n\n  // Create merged changeset content\n  const mergedContent = createMergedChangeset(highestBumpType, descriptions);\n\n  // Generate a unique name for the merged changeset\n  const mergedFileName = `merged-${generateChangesetName()}.md`;\n  const mergedFilePath = join(CHANGESET_DIR, mergedFileName);\n\n  // Write the merged changeset\n  writeFileSync(mergedFilePath, mergedContent);\n  console.log(`\\nCreated merged changeset: ${mergedFileName}`);\n\n  // Remove the original changeset files\n  console.log('\\nRemoving original changeset files:');\n  for (const changeset of parsedChangesets) {\n    unlinkSync(changeset.filePath);\n    console.log(`  Removed: ${changeset.file}`);\n  }\n\n  console.log('\\nChangeset merge completed successfully');\n  console.log(`\\nMerged changeset content:\\n${mergedContent}`);\n}\n\nmain();\n",

<path>]\n *   should_pull: Optional flag to pull latest changes before publishing (for release job)\n *\n * IMPORTANT: Update the PACKAGE_NAME constant below to match your package.json\n *\n * Configuration:\n * - CLI: --js-root <path> to explicitly set JavaScript root\n * - Environment: JS_ROOT=<path>\n *\n * Uses link-foundation libraries:\n * - use-m: Dynamic package loading without package.json dependencies\n * - command-stream: Modern shell command execution with streaming support\n * - lino-arguments: Unified configuration from CLI args, env vars, and .lenv files\n *\n * process.chdir()\nconst originalCwd = process.cwd();\n\n// Patterns that indicate publish failure in changeset output\n// Reference: link-assistant/agent PR #116 - prevent false positives in CI/CD\nconst FAILURE_PATTERNS = [\n  'packages failed to publish',\n  'error occurred while publishing',\n  'npm error code E',\n  'npm error 404',\n  'npm error 401',\n  'npm error 403',\n  'Access token expired',\n  'ENEEDAUTH',\n];\n\n/**\n * Sleep for specified milliseconds\n * @param {number} ms\n */\nfunction sleep(ms) {\n  return new Promise((resolve) => globalThis.setTimeout(resolve, ms));\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if the output contains any failure patterns\n * Reference: link-assistant/agent PR #116\n * @param {string} output - Combined stdout and stderr\n * @returns {string|null} - The matched failure pattern or null if no failure detected\n */\nfunction detectPublishFailure(output) {\n  const lowerOutput = output.toLowerCase();\n  for (const pattern of FAILURE_PATTERNS) {\n    if (lowerOutput.includes(pattern.toLowerCase())) {\n      return pattern;\n    }\n  }\n  return null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Verify that a package version is published on npm\n * Reference: link-assistant/agent PR #116\n * @param {string} packageName\n * @param {string} version\n * @returns {Promise<boolean>}\n */\nasync function verifyPublished(packageName, version) {\n  const result = function runChangesetPublish() {\n  try {\n    // Run changeset:publish from the js directory where package.json with this script exists\n    // IMPORTANT: Use .run({ capture: true }) to capture output for failure detection\n    // IMPORTANT: cd is a virtual command that calls process.chdir(), so we restore after\n    if (needsCd({ jsRoot })) {\n      const result = await $`cd ${jsRoot} && null otherwise\n */\nfunction analyzePublishResult(publishResult, commandError) {\n  if (commandError) {\n    return commandError;\n  }\n\n  const combinedOutput = publishResult\n    ? `${publishResult.stdout || ''}\\n${publishResult.stderr || ''}`\n    : '';\n\n  // Log the output for debugging\n  if (combinedOutput.trim()) {\n    console.log('Changeset output:', combinedOutput);\n publish failure: \"${failurePattern}\"`);\n    return new Error(`Publish failed: detected \"${failurePattern}\" in output`);\n  }\n\n  // Check exit code (if available and non-zero)\n  if (publishResult && publishResult.code !== 0) {\n    console.error(`Changeset exited with code ${publishResult.code}`);\n    return new Error(`Publish failed with exit code ${publishResult.code}`);\n  }\n\n  return null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Perform a single publish attempt with verification\n * @param {string} currentVersion\n * @returns {Promise<{success: boolean, error: Error|null}>}\n */\nasync function attemptPublish(currentVersion) {\n  const { result, error } = await runChangesetPublish();\n  const analysisError = analyzePublishResult(result, error);\n\n  if (analysisError) {\n    return // Wait for npm registry to propagate\n  const isPublished = await verifyPublished(PACKAGE_NAME, currentVersion);\n\n  if (isPublished) {\n    return { success: true, error: null };\n  }\n\n  console.error('Verification failed: package not found on npm after publish');\n  return {\n    success: false,\n    error: new Error('Package not found on npm after publish attempt'),\n  };\n}\n\nasync function main() {\n  try {\n    if (shouldPull) {\n      // Pull the latest changes we just pushed\n      await $`git pull origin main`;\n    }\n\n    // Get current version\n    const packageJsonPath = getPackageJsonPath({ jsRoot });\n    const packageJson = JSON.parse(readFileSync(packageJsonPath, 'utf8'));\n    const currentVersion = packageJson.version;\n    console.log(`Current version to publish: ${currentVersion}`);\n\n    // Check if this version is already published on npm\n    console.log(\n      `Checking if version ${currentVersion} is already published...`\n    );\n    const checkResult =\n      await $`npm view \"${PACKAGE_NAME}@${currentVersion}\" version`.run({\n        capture: true,\n      });\n\n    // command-stream returns { code: 0 } on success, { code: 1 } on failure (e.g., E404)\n    // Exit code 0 means version exists, non-zero means version not found\n    if (checkResult.code === 0) {\n      console.log(`Version ${currentVersion} is already published to npm`);\n      setOutput('published', 'true');\n      setOutput('published_version', currentVersion);\n      setOutput('already_published', 'true');\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Version not found on npm (E404), proceed with publish\n    console.log(\n      `Version ${currentVersion} not found on npm, proceeding with publish...`\n    );\n\n    // Publish to npm using OIDC trusted publishing with retry logic\n    // Multi-layer failure detection based on link-assistant/agent PR #116\n    for (let i = 1; i <= MAX_RETRIES; i++) {\n      console.log(`Publish attempt ${i} of ${MAX_RETRIES}...`);\n      const { success, error } = await attemptPublish(currentVersion);\n\n      if (success) {\n        setOutput('published', 'true');\n        setOutput('published_version', currentVersion);\n        console.log(\n          `\\u2705 Published ${PACKAGE_NAME}@${currentVersion} to npm`\n        );\n        return;\n      }\n\n      if (i < MAX_RETRIES) {\n        console.log(\n          `Publish failed: ${error.message}, waiting ${RETRY_DELAY / 1000}s before retry...`\n        );\n        await sleep(RETRY_DELAY);\n      }\n    }\n\n    console.error(`\\u274C Failed to publish after ${MAX_RETRIES} attempts`);\n    process.exit(1);\n  } catch (error) {\n    // Restore cwd on error\n    process.chdir(originalCwd);\n    console.error('Error:', error.message);\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n}\n\nmain();\n",

[2026-05-01T08:53:55.293Z] [INFO]     "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env bun\n\n/**\n * Validate changeset for CI - ensures exactly one valid changeset is added by the PR\n *\n * Key behavior:\n * - Only checks changeset files ADDED by the current PR (not pre-existing ones)\n * - Uses git diff to compare PR head against base branch\n * - Validates that the PR adds exactly one changeset with proper format\n * - Falls back to checking all changesets for local development\n *\n * IMPORTANT: Update the package name below to match your package.json\n */\n\nimport { execSync } from 'child_process';\nimport { readFileSync, readdirSync, existsSync } from 'fs';\nimport { join } from 'path';\n\n// TODO: Update this to match your package name in package.json\nconst PACKAGE_NAME = 'my-package';\nconst CHANGESET_DIR = '.changeset';\n\n/**\n * Ensure a git commit is available locally, fetching if necessary\n * @param {string} sha The commit SHA to check\n */\nfunction ensureCommitAvailable(sha) {\n  try {\n    execSync(`git cat-file -e ${sha}`, { stdio: 'ignore' });\n  } catch {\n    console.log('Base commit not available locally, attempting fetch...');\n    try {\n      execSync(`git fetch origin ${sha}`, { stdio: 'inherit' });\n    } catch {\n      execSync(`git fetch origin`, { stdio: 'inherit' });\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse git diff output and extract added changeset files\n * @param {string} diffOutput Output from git diff --name-status\n * @returns {string[]} Array of added changeset file names\n */\nfunction parseAddedChangesets(diffOutput) {\n  const addedChangesets = [];\n  for (const line of diffOutput.trim().split('\\n')) {\n    if (!line) {\n      continue;\n    }\n    const [status, filePath] = line.split('\\t');\n    if (\n      status === 'A' &&\n      filePath.startsWith(`${CHANGESET_DIR}/`) &&\n      filePath.endsWith('.md') &&\n      !filePath.endsWith('README.md')\n    ) {\n      addedChangesets.push(filePath.replace(`${CHANGESET_DIR}/`, ''));\n    }\n  }\n  return addedChangesets;\n}\n\n/**\n * Try to get changesets using explicit SHA comparison\n * @param {string} baseSha Base commit SHA\n * @param {string} headSha Head commit SHA\n * @returns {string[] | null} Array of changeset files or null if failed\n */\nfunction tryExplicitShaComparison(baseSha, headSha) {\n  console.log(`Comparing ${baseSha}...${headSha}`);\n  try {\n    ensureCommitAvailable(baseSha);\n    const diffOutput = execSync(\n      `git diff --name-status ${baseSha} ${headSha}`,\n      { encoding: 'utf-8' }\n    );\n    return parseAddedChangesets(diffOutput);\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.log(`Git diff with explicit SHAs failed: ${error.message}`);\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Try to get changesets using base branch comparison\n * @param {string} prBase Base branch name\n * @returns {string[] | null} Array of changeset files or null if failed\n */\nfunction tryBaseBranchComparison(prBase) {\n  console.log(`Comparing against base branch: ${prBase}`);\n  try {\n    try {\n      execSync(`git fetch origin ${prBase}`, { stdio: 'inherit' });\n    } catch {\n      // Ignore fetch errors, we might already have it\n    }\n    const diffOutput = execSync(\n      `git diff --name-status origin/${prBase}...HEAD`,\n      { encoding: 'utf-8' }\n    );\n    return parseAddedChangesets(diffOutput);\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.log(`Git diff with base ref failed: ${error.message}`);\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Fallback: get all changesets in directory\n * @returns {string[]} Array of all changeset file names\n */\nfunction getAllChangesets() {\n  console.log(\n    'Warning: Could not determine PR diff, checking all changesets in directory'\n  );\n  if (!existsSync(CHANGESET_DIR)) {\n    return [];\n  }\n  return readdirSync(CHANGESET_DIR).filter(\n    (file) => file.endsWith('.md') && file !== 'README.md'\n  );\n}\n\n/**\n * Get changeset files added in the current PR using git diff\n * @returns {string[]} Array of added changeset file names\n */\nfunction getAddedChangesetFiles() {\n  const baseSha = process.env.GITHUB_BASE_SHA || process.env.BASE_SHA;\n  const headSha = process.env.GITHUB_HEAD_SHA || process.env.HEAD_SHA;\n\n  // Try explicit SHAs first\n  if (baseSha && headSha) {\n    const result = tryExplicitShaComparison(baseSha, headSha);\n    if (result !== null) {\n      return result;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Try base branch comparison\n  const prBase = process.env.GITHUB_BASE_REF;\n  if (prBase) {\n    const result = tryBaseBranchComparison(prBase);\n    if (result !== null) {\n      return result;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Fallback to checking all changesets\n  return getAllChangesets();\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate a single changeset file\n * @param {string} filePath Full path to the changeset file\n * @returns {{valid: boolean, type?: string, description?: string, error?: string}}\n */\nfunction validateChangesetFile(filePath) {\n  try {\n    const content = readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8');\n\n    // Check if changeset has a valid type (major, minor, or patch)\n    const versionTypeRegex = new RegExp(\n      `^['\"]${PACKAGE_NAME.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\\]\\\\]/g, '\\\\$&')}['\"]:\\\\s+(major|minor|patch)`,\n      'm'\n    );\n    const versionTypeMatch = content.match(versionTypeRegex);\n\n    if (!versionTypeMatch) {\n      return {\n        valid: false,\n        error: `Changeset must specify a version type: major, minor, or patch\\nExpected format:\\n---\\n'${PACKAGE_NAME}': patch\\n---\\n\\nYour description here`,\n      };\n    }\n\n    // Extract description (everything after the closing ---) and check it's not empty\n    const parts = content.split('---');\n    if (parts.length < 3) {\n      return {\n        valid: false,\n        error:\n          \"Changeset must include a description of the changes (after the closing '---')\",\n      };\n    }\n\n    const description = parts.slice(2).join('---').trim();\n    if (!description) {\n      return {\n        valid: false,\n        error: 'Changeset must include a non-empty description of the changes',\n      };\n    }\n\n    return {\n      valid: true,\n      type: versionTypeMatch[1],\n      description,\n    };\n  } catch (error) {\n    return {\n      valid: false,\n      error: `Failed to read changeset file: ${error.message}`,\n    };\n  }\n}\n\ntry {\n  console.log('Validating changesets added by this PR...');\n\n  // Get changeset files added in this PR\n  const addedChangesetFiles = getAddedChangesetFiles();\n  const changesetCount = addedChangesetFiles.length;\n\n  console.log(`Found ${changesetCount} changeset file(s) added by this PR`);\n  if (changesetCount > 0) {\n    console.log('Added changesets:');\n    addedChangesetFiles.forEach((file) => console.log(`  - ${file}`));\n  }\n\n  // Ensure exactly one changeset file was added\n  if (changesetCount === 0) {\n    console.error(\n      \"::error::No changeset found in this PR. Please add a changeset by running 'npm run changeset' and commit the result.\"\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  } else if (changesetCount > 1) {\n    console.error(\n      `::error::Multiple changesets found in this PR (${changesetCount}). Each PR should add exactly ONE changeset.`\n    );\n    console.error('::error::Found changeset files added by this PR:');\n    addedChangesetFiles.forEach((file) => console.error(`  ${file}`));\n    console.error(\n      '\\n::error::Please combine these into a single changeset or remove the extras.'\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  // Validate the single changeset file\n  const changesetFile = join(CHANGESET_DIR, addedChangesetFiles[0]);\n  console.log(`Validating changeset: ${changesetFile}`);\n\n  const validation = validateChangesetFile(changesetFile);\n\n  if (!validation.valid) {\n    console.error(`::error::${validation.error}`);\n    console.error(`\\nFile content of ${changesetFile}:`);\n    try {\n      console.error(readFileSync(changesetFile, 'utf-8'));\n    } catch {\n      console.error('(could not read file)');\n    }\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  console.log('Changeset validation passed');\n  console.log(`   Type: ${validation.type}`);\n  console.log(`   Description: ${validation.description}`);\n} catch (error) {\n  console.error('Error during changeset validation:', error.message);\n  if (process.env.DEBUG) {\n    console.error('Stack trace:', error.stack);\n  }\n  process.exit(1);\n}\n",

Click \"Use this template\" on GitHub to create a new repository\n2. Clone your new repository\n3. Update `package.json` with your package name and description\n4. Update the `PACKAGE_NAME` constant in these scripts:\n   - `scripts/validate-changeset.mjs`\n   - `scripts/merge-changesets.mjs`\n   - `scripts/publish-to-npm.mjs`\n   - `scripts/format-release-notes.mjs`\n   - `scripts/create-manual-changeset.mjs`\n5. Install dependencies: `bun install`\n6. Start developing!",
[2026-05-01T08:54:21.984Z] [INFO]           "new_string": "# meta-sovereign\n\nA personal meta profile **sovereign** system. Fully local, privacy-respecting. The user actually owns and controls their data about their network of contacts, connections, and partners — across every social, messenger, and job-board network they use.\n\nTracked by issue [#1 — Prototype version 0.0.1](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-sovereign/issues/1). The full case study, requirements list, architecture sketch, and phased solution plan live in [`docs/case-studies/issue-1/`](docs/case-studies/issue-1/README.md).\n\n## What it will do (target capabilities)\n\n- **Unified inbox** spanning VK, Telegram, X, WhatsApp, Facebook, LinkedIn, career.habr.com, hh.ru, superjob.ru.\n- **Personal CRM**: contacts, communities, group memberships, intersections, mass-personal outreach.\n- **Personal memory**: structured `question → answer` facts captured automatically from conversations.\n- **Conversation automation platform**: pattern editors, reply-variation editors, n8n-style dialog graphs.\n- **Portable data store**: dual binary ([Doublets](https://github.com/linksplatform/doublets-rs)) + text ([Links Notation](https://github.com/link-foundation/links-notation)) representation, automated backups, `.lino` import/export.\n- **Local-first runtime**: WebRTC sync between user-owned devices, optional self-hosted personal cloud.\n- **Two stacks**: JS + Rust/WebAssembly (default) and pure Rust (server/microservice variant). The on-disk format is shared.\n\nThis repository is built on top of [`link-foundation/js-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template`](https://github.com/link-foundation/js-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template) and inherits its CI/CD jobs. The current `src/` is a placeholder; feature implementation will land milestone-by-milestone per [`solution-plan.md`](docs/case-studies/issue-1/solution-plan.md).\n\n## Status\n\n- **0.0.0**: scaffolding inherited from the JS template.\n- **0.0.1 (in progress, this PR #2)**: case study, requirements, solution plan, repository identity.\n- **0.0.2 → 0.0.x**: storage skeleton, importer adapters, UI surfaces — see [Solution Plan](docs/case-studies/issue-1/solution-plan.md).\n\n## Inherited features (CI/CD baseline)\n\n- **Multi-runtime support**: Works with Bun, Node.js, and Deno\n- **Universal testing**: Uses [test-anywhere](https://github.com/link-foundation/test-anywhere) for cross-runtime tests\n- **Automated releases**: Changesets-based versioning with GitHub Actions\n- **Code quality**: ESLint + Prettier with pre-commit hooks via Husky\n- **Package manager agnostic**: Works with bun, npm, yarn, pnpm, and deno\n- **Broken link checks**: Automated link validation with [lychee](https://github.com/lycheeverse/lychee-action) and Web Archive fallback suggestions\n\n## Quick Start"
