[2026-05-01T08:54:21.997Z] [INFO]     "originalFile": "# js-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template\n\nA comprehensive template for AI-driven JavaScript/TypeScript development with full CI/CD pipeline support.\n\n## Features\n\n- **Multi-runtime support**: Works with Bun, Node.js, and Deno\n- **Universal testing**: Uses [test-anywhere](https://github.com/link-foundation/test-anywhere) for cross-runtime tests\n- **Automated releases**: Changesets-based versioning with GitHub Actions\n- **Code quality**: ESLint + Prettier with pre-commit hooks via Husky\n- **Package manager agnostic**: Works with bun, npm, yarn, pnpm, and deno\n- **Broken link checks**: Automated link validation with [lychee](https://github.com/lycheeverse/lychee-action) and Web Archive fallback suggestions\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### Using This Template\n\n1. Click \"Use this template\" on GitHub to create a new repository\n2. Clone your new repository\n3. Update `package.json` with your package name and description\n4. Update the `PACKAGE_NAME` constant in these scripts:\n   - `scripts/validate-changeset.mjs`\n   - `scripts/merge-changesets.mjs`\n   - `scripts/publish-to-npm.mjs`\n   - `scripts/format-release-notes.mjs`\n   - `scripts/create-manual-changeset.mjs`\n5. Install dependencies: `bun install`\n6. Start developing!\n\n### Development\n\n```bash\n# Install dependencies\nbun install\n\n# Run tests\nbun test\n\n# Or with other runtimes:\nnpm test\ndeno test --allow-read\n\n# Lint code\nbun run lint\n\n# Format code\nbun run format\n\n# Check all (lint + format + file size)\nbun run check\n```\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\n.\n├── .changeset/           # Changeset configuration\n├── .github/workflows/    # GitHub Actions CI/CD\n├── .husky/               # Git hooks (pre-commit)\n├── examples/             # Usage examples\n├── scripts/              # Build and release scripts\n├── src/                  # Source code\n│   ├── index.js          # Main entry point\n│   └── index.d.ts        # TypeScript definitions\n├── tests/                # Test files\n├── .eslintrc.js          # ESLint configuration\n├── .prettierrc           # Prettier configuration\n├── bunfig.toml           # Bun configuration\n├── deno.json             # Deno configuration\n└── package.json          # Node.js package manifest\n```\n\n## Design Choices\n\n### Multi-Runtime Support\n\nThis template is designed to work seamlessly with all major JavaScript runtimes:\n\n- **Bun**: Primary runtime with highest performance, uses native test support (`bun test`)\n- **Node.js**: Alternative runtime, uses built-in test runner (`node --test`)\n- **Deno**: Secure runtime with built-in TypeScript support (`deno test`)\n\nThe [test-anywhere](https://github.com/link-foundation/test-anywhere) framework provides a unified testing API that works identically across all runtimes.\n\n### Package Manager Agnostic\n\nWhile `package.json` is the source of truth for dependencies, the template supports:\n\n- **bun**: Primary choice, uses `bun.lockb`\n- **npm**: Uses `package-lock.json`\n- **yarn**: Uses `yarn.lock`\n- **pnpm**: Uses `pnpm-lock.yaml`\n- **deno**: Uses `deno.json` for configuration\n\nNote: `package-lock.json` is not committed by default to allow any package manager.\n\n### Code Quality\n\n- **ESLint**: Configured with recommended rules + Prettier integration\n- **Prettier**: Consistent code formatting\n- **Husky + lint-staged**: Pre-commit hooks ensure code quality\n- **File size limit**: Files must stay under 1500 lines for maintainability (enforced via ESLint and CI)\n\n### Release Workflow\n\nThe release workflow uses [Changesets](https://github.com/changesets/changesets) for version management:\n\n1. **Creating a changeset**: Run `bun run changeset` to document changes\n2. **PR validation**: CI checks for valid changeset in each PR\n3. **Automated versioning**: Merging to `main` triggers version bump\n4. **npm publishing**: Automated via OIDC trusted publishing (no tokens needed)\n5. **GitHub releases**: Auto-created with formatted release notes\n\n#### Manual Releases\n\nTwo manual release modes are available via GitHub Actions:\n\n- **Instant release**: Immediately bump version and publish\n- **Changeset PR**: Create a PR with changeset for review\n\n### CI/CD Pipeline\n\nThe GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/release.yml`) implements a fast-fail pipeline:\n\n**Fast checks** (~7-30s each, run first for fastest feedback):\n\n1. **Test compilation**: Syntax-checks all `.mjs` files with `node --check`\n2. **Lint, format & secrets scan**: ESLint, Prettier, jscpd, and [secretlint](https://github.com/secretlint/secretlint) for credential leak detection\n3. **File line limits**: Enforces 1500-line limit on `.mjs` files and `release.yml`\n4. **Changeset check**: Validates PR has exactly one changeset (added by that PR)\n5. **Version check**: Blocks manual version changes in `package.json`\n6. **Documentation validation**: Checks doc file sizes and required files\n\n**Slow checks** (only run after all fast checks pass):\n\n7. **Test matrix**: 3 runtimes × 3 OS = 9 test combinations\n8. **Broken link checks**: Validates all links in Markdown/HTML files (separate workflow)\n\n**Release** (on merge to main):\n\n9. **Changeset merge**: Combines multiple pending changesets at release time\n10. **Release**: Automated versioning and npm publishing\n\nSee [BEST-PRACTICES.md](docs/BEST-PRACTICES.md) for detailed explanations of each practice.\n\n#### Robust Changeset Handling\n\nThe CI/CD pipeline is designed to handle concurrent PRs gracefully:\n\n- **PR Validation**: Only validates changesets **added by the current PR**, not pre-existing ones from other merged PRs. This prevents false failures when multiple PRs merge before a release cycle completes.\n\n- **Release-time Merging**: If multiple changesets exist when releasing, they are automatically merged into a single changeset with:\n  - The highest version bump type (major > minor > patch)\n  - All descriptions preserved in chronological order\n\nThis design decouples PR validation from the need to pull changes from the default branch, reducing conflicts and ensuring that even if CI/CD fails, all unpublished changesets will still get published when the error is resolved.\n\n### Broken Link Checker\n\nThe link checker workflow (`.github/workflows/links.yml`) validates all links in Markdown and HTML files:\n\n1. **Detection**: Uses [lychee](https://github.com/lycheeverse/lychee-action) to scan all `*.md` and `*.html` files\n2. **Web Archive fallback**: For any broken links found, automatically checks the [Wayback Machine](https://web.archive.org) for archived versions\n3. **Actionable suggestions**: Reports one of three outcomes for each broken link:\n   - **Archived**: Suggests the Web Archive URL as a replacement\n   - **Not archived**: Clearly reports the link is unrecoverable\n4. **Scheduled checks**: Runs weekly to catch links that break over time (even if no files changed)\n5. **Issue creation**: On scheduled runs, creates a GitHub Issue with the full broken links report\n\nAdd regex patterns to `.lycheeignore` to exclude URLs from checks (e.g., local dev URLs, example.com, known rate-limited sites).\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Updating Package Name\n\nAfter creating a repository from this template, update the package name in:\n\n1. `package.json`: `\"name\": \"your-package-name\"`\n2. `.changeset/config.json`: Package references\n3. Scripts that reference the package name (see Quick Start)\n\n### ESLint Rules\n\nCustomize ESLint in `eslint.config.js`. Current configuration:\n\n- ES Modules support\n- Prettier integration\n- No console restrictions (common in CLI tools)\n- Strict equality enforcement\n- Async/await best practices\n- **Strict unused variables rule**: No exceptions - all unused variables, arguments, and caught errors must be removed (no `_` prefix exceptions)\n\n### Prettier Options\n\nConfigured in `.prettierrc`:\n\n- Single quotes\n- Semicolons\n- 2-space indentation\n- 80-character line width\n- ES5 trailing commas\n- LF line endings\n\n## Scripts Reference\n\n| Script                 | Description                             |\n| ---------------------- | --------------------------------------- |\n| `bun test`             | Run tests with Bun                      |\n| `bun run lint`         | Check code with ESLint                  |\n| `bun run lint:fix`     | Fix ESLint issues automatically         |\n| `bun run format`       | Format code with Prettier               |\n| `bun run format:check` | Check formatting without changing files |\n| `bun run check`        | Run all checks (lint + format)          |\n| `bun run changeset`    | Create a new changeset                  |\n\n## Contributing\n\nSee [CONTRIBUTING.md](docs/CONTRIBUTING.md) for detailed contribution guidelines.\n\nQuick steps:\n\n1. Fork the repository\n2. Create a feature branch: `git checkout -b feature/my-feature`\n3. Make your changes\n4. Create a changeset: `bun run changeset`\n5. Commit your changes (pre-commit hooks will run automatically)\n6. Push and create a Pull Request\n\n## Best Practices\n\nThis template implements CI/CD best practices for AI-driven development. See [BEST-PRACTICES.md](docs/BEST-PRACTICES.md) for details on:\n\n- File size limits for AI readability\n- Automated formatting and linting\n- Multi-runtime and cross-platform testing\n- Changeset-based versioning\n- Concurrency control for CI/CD pipelines\n\n## License\n\n[Unlicense](LICENSE) - Public Domain\n",

[2026-05-01T08:54:24.357Z] [INFO]       "content": "\n- **Multi-runtime support**: Works with Bun, Node.js, and Deno\n- **Universal testing**: Uses [test-anywhere](https://github.com/link-foundation/test-anywhere) for cross-runtime tests\n- **Automated releases**: Changesets-based versioning with GitHub Actions\n- **Code quality**: ESLint + Prettier with pre-commit hooks via Husky\n- **Package manager agnostic**: Works with bun, npm, yarn, pnpm, and deno\n- **Broken link checks**: Automated link validation with [lychee](https://github.com/lycheeverse/lychee-action) and Web Archive fallback suggestions\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### Development\n\n```bash\n# Install dependencies\nbun install\n\n# Run tests\nbun test\n\n# Or with other runtimes:\nnpm test\ndeno test --allow-read\n\n# Lint code\nbun run lint\n\n# Format code\nbun run format\n\n# Check all (lint + format + file size)\nbun run check\n```\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\n.\n├── .changeset/           # Changeset configuration\n├── .github/workflows/    # GitHub Actions CI/CD\n├── .husky/               # Git hooks (pre-commit)\n├── examples/             # Usage examples\n├── scripts/              # Build and release scripts\n├── src/                  # Source code\n│   ├── index.js          # Main entry point\n│   └── index.d.ts        # TypeScript definitions\n├── tests/                # Test files\n├── .eslintrc.js          # ESLint configuration\n├── .prettierrc           # Prettier configuration\n├── bunfig.toml           # Bun configuration\n├── deno.json             # Deno configuration\n└── package.json          # Node.js package manifest\n```\n\n## Design Choices\n\n### Multi-Runtime Support\n\nThis template is designed to work seamlessly with all major JavaScript runtimes:\n\n- **Bun**: Primary runtime with highest performance, uses native test support (`bun test`)\n- **Node.js**: Alternative runtime, uses built-in test runner (`node --test`)\n- **Deno**: Secure runtime with built-in TypeScript support (`deno test`)\n\nThe [test-anywhere](https://github.com/link-foundation/test-anywhere) framework provides a unified testing API that works identically across all runtimes.\n\n### Package Manager Agnostic\n\nWhile `package.json` is the source of truth for dependencies, the template supports:\n\n- **bun**: Primary choice, uses `bun.lockb`\n- **npm**: Uses `package-lock.json`\n- **yarn**: Uses `yarn.lock`\n- **pnpm**: Uses `pnpm-lock.yaml`\n- **deno**: Uses `deno.json` for configuration\n\nNote: `package-lock.json` is not committed by default to allow any package manager.\n\n### Code Quality\n\n- **ESLint**: Configured with recommended rules + Prettier integration\n- **Prettier**: Consistent code formatting\n- **Husky + lint-staged**: Pre-commit hooks ensure code quality\n- **File size limit**: Files must stay under 1500 lines for maintainability (enforced via ESLint and CI)\n\n### Release Workflow\n\nThe release workflow uses [Changesets](https://github.com/changesets/changesets) for version management:\n\n1. **Creating a changeset**: Run `bun run changeset` to document changes\n2. **PR validation**: CI checks for valid changeset in each PR",

bump\n117\t4. **npm publishing**: Automated via OIDC trusted publishing (no tokens needed)\n118\t5. **GitHub releases**: Auto-created with formatted release notes\n119\t\n120\t#### Manual Releases\n121\t\n122\tTwo manual release modes are available via GitHub Actions:\n123\t\n124\t- **Instant release**: Immediately bump version and publish\n125\t- **Changeset PR**: Create a PR with changeset for review\n126\t\n127\t### CI/CD Pipeline\n128\t\n129\tThe GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/release.yml`) implements a fast-fail pipeline:\n130\t\n131\t**Fast checks** (~7-30s each, run first for fastest feedback):\n132\t\n133\t1. **Test compilation**: Syntax-checks all `.mjs` files with `node --check`\n134\t2. **Lint, format & secrets scan**: ESLint, Prettier, jscpd, and [secretlint](https://github.com/secretlint/secretlint) for credential leak detection\n135\t3. **File line limits**: Enforces 1500-line limit on `.mjs` files and `release.yml`\n136\t4. **Changeset check**: Validates PR has exactly one changeset (added by that PR)\n137\t5. **Version check**: Blocks manual version changes in `package.json`\n138\t6. **Documentation validation**: Checks doc file sizes and required files\n139\t\n140\t**Slow checks** (only run after all fast checks pass):\n141\t\n142\t7. **Test matrix**: 3 runtimes × 3 OS = 9 test combinations\n143\t8. **Broken link checks**: Validates all links in Markdown/HTML files (separate workflow)\n144\t\n145\t**Release** (on merge to main):\n146\t\n147\t9. **Changeset merge**: Combines multiple pending changesets at release time\n148\t10. **Release**: Automated versioning and npm publishing\n149\t\n150\tSee [BEST-PRACTICES.md](docs/BEST-PRACTICES.md) for detailed explanations of each practice.\n151\t\n152\t#### Robust Changeset Handling\n153\t\n154\tThe CI/CD pipeline is designed to handle concurrent PRs gracefully:\n155\t\n156\t- **PR Validation**: Only validates changesets **added by the current PR**, not pre-existing ones from other merged PRs. This prevents false failures when multiple PRs merge before a release cycle completes.\n157\t\n158\t- **Release-time Merging**: If multiple changesets exist when releasing, they are automatically merged into a single changeset with:\n159\t  - The highest version bump type (major > minor > patch)\n160\t  - All descriptions preserved in chronological order\n161\t\n162\tThis design decouples PR validation from the need to pull changes from the default branch, reducing conflicts and ensuring that even if CI/CD fails, all unpublished changesets will still get published when the error is resolved.\n163\t\n164\t### Broken Link Checker\n165\t\n166\tThe link checker workflow (`.github/workflows/links.yml`) validates all links in Markdown and HTML files:\n167\t\n168\t1. **Detection**: Uses [lychee](https://github.com/lycheeverse/lychee-action) to scan all `*.md` and `*.html` files\n169\t2. **Web Archive fallback**: For any broken links found, automatically checks the [Wayback Machine](https://web.archive.org) for archived versions\n170\t3. **Actionable suggestions**: Reports one of three outcomes for each broken link:\n171\t   - **Archived**: Suggests the Web Archive URL as a replacement\n172\t   - **Not archived**: Clearly reports the link is unrecoverable\n173\t4. **Scheduled checks**: Runs weekly to catch links that break over time (even if no files changed)\n174\t5. **Issue creation**: On scheduled runs, creates a GitHub Issue with the full broken links report\n175\t\n176\tAdd regex patterns to `.lycheeignore` to exclude URLs from checks (e.g., local dev URLs, example.com, known rate-limited sites).\n177\t\n178\t## Configuration\n179\t\n180\t### Updating Package Name\n181\t\n182\tAfter creating a repository from this template, update the package name in:\n183\t\n184\t1. `package.json`: `\"name\": \"your-package-name\"`\n185\t2. `.changeset/config.json`: Package references\n186\t3. Scripts that reference the package name (see Quick Start)\n187\t\n188\t### ESLint Rules\n189\t\n190\tCustomize ESLint in `eslint.config.js`. Current configuration:\n191\t\n192\t- ES Modules support\n193\t- Prettier integration\n194\t- No console restrictions (common in CLI tools)"

[2026-05-01T08:54:27.969Z] [INFO]       "content": "2. **PR validation**: CI checks for valid changeset in each PR\n3. **Automated versioning**: Merging to `main` triggers version bump\n4. **npm publishing**: Automated via OIDC trusted publishing (no tokens needed)\n5. **GitHub releases**: Auto-created with formatted release notes\n\n#### Manual Releases\n\nTwo manual release modes are available via GitHub Actions:\n\n- **Instant release**: Immediately bump version and publish\n- **Changeset PR**: Create a PR with changeset for review\n\n### CI/CD Pipeline\n\nThe GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/release.yml`) implements a fast-fail pipeline:\n\n**Fast checks** (~7-30s each, run first for fastest feedback):\n\n1. **Test compilation**: Syntax-checks all `.mjs` files with `node --check`\n2. **Lint, format & secrets scan**: ESLint, Prettier, jscpd, and [secretlint](https://github.com/secretlint/secretlint) for credential leak detection\n3. **File line limits**: Enforces 1500-line limit on `.mjs` files and `release.yml`\n4. **Changeset check**: Validates PR has exactly one changeset (added by that PR)\n5. **Version check**: Blocks manual version changes in `package.json`\n6. **Documentation validation**: Checks doc file sizes and required files\n\n**Slow checks** (only run after all fast checks pass):\n\n7. **Test matrix**: 3 runtimes × 3 OS = 9 test combinations\n8. **Broken link checks**: Validates all links in Markdown/HTML files (separate workflow)\n\n**Release** (on merge to main):\n\n9. **Changeset merge**: Combines multiple pending changesets at release time\n10. **Release**: Automated versioning and npm publishing\n\nSee [BEST-PRACTICES.md](docs/BEST-PRACTICES.md) for detailed explanations of each practice.\n\n#### Robust Changeset Handling\n\nThe CI/CD pipeline is designed to handle concurrent PRs gracefully:\n\n- **PR Validation**: Only validates changesets **added by the current PR**, not pre-existing ones from other merged PRs. This prevents false failures when multiple PRs merge before a release cycle completes.\n\n- **Release-time Merging**: If multiple changesets exist when releasing, they are automatically merged into a single changeset with:\n  - The highest version bump type (major > minor > patch)\n  - All descriptions preserved in chronological order\n\nThis design decouples PR validation from the need to pull changes from the default branch, reducing conflicts and ensuring that even if CI/CD fails, all unpublished changesets will still get published when the error is resolved.\n\n### Broken Link Checker\n\nThe link checker workflow (`.github/workflows/links.yml`) validates all links in Markdown and HTML files:\n\n1. **Detection**: Uses [lychee](https://github.com/lycheeverse/lychee-action) to scan all `*.md` and `*.html` files\n2. **Web Archive fallback**: For any broken links found, automatically checks the [Wayback Machine](https://web.archive.org) for archived versions\n3. **Actionable suggestions**: Reports one of three outcomes for each broken link:\n   - **Archived**: Suggests the Web Archive URL as a replacement\n   - **Not archived**: Clearly reports the link is unrecoverable\n4. **Scheduled checks**: Runs weekly to catch links that break over time (even if no files changed)\n5. **Issue creation**: On scheduled runs, creates a GitHub Issue with the full broken links report\n\nAdd regex patterns to `.lycheeignore` to exclude URLs from checks (e.g., local dev URLs, example.com, known rate-limited sites).\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Updating Package Name\n\nAfter creating a repository from this template, update the package name in:\n\n1. `package.json`: `\"name\": \"your-package-name\"`\n2. `.changeset/config.json`: Package references\n3. Scripts that reference the package name (see Quick Start)\n\n### ESLint Rules\n\nCustomize ESLint in `eslint.config.js`. Current configuration:\n\n- ES Modules support\n- Prettier integration\n- No console restrictions (common in CLI tools)",

[2026-05-01T08:54:34.444Z] [INFO]     "originalFile": "# meta-sovereign\n\nA personal meta profile **sovereign** system. Fully local, privacy-respecting. The user actually owns and controls their data about their network of contacts, connections, and partners — across every social, messenger, and job-board network they use.\n\nTracked by issue [#1 — Prototype version 0.0.1](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-sovereign/issues/1). The full case study, requirements list, architecture sketch, and phased solution plan live in [`docs/case-studies/issue-1/`](docs/case-studies/issue-1/README.md).\n\n## What it will do (target capabilities)\n\n- **Unified inbox** spanning VK, Telegram, X, WhatsApp, Facebook, LinkedIn, career.habr.com, hh.ru, superjob.ru.\n- **Personal CRM**: contacts, communities, group memberships, intersections, mass-personal outreach.\n- **Personal memory**: structured `question → answer` facts captured automatically from conversations.\n- **Conversation automation platform**: pattern editors, reply-variation editors, n8n-style dialog graphs.\n- **Portable data store**: dual binary ([Doublets](https://github.com/linksplatform/doublets-rs)) + text ([Links Notation](https://github.com/link-foundation/links-notation)) representation, automated backups, `.lino` import/export.\n- **Local-first runtime**: WebRTC sync between user-owned devices, optional self-hosted personal cloud.\n- **Two stacks**: JS + Rust/WebAssembly (default) and pure Rust (server/microservice variant). The on-disk format is shared.\n\nThis repository is built on top of [`link-foundation/js-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template`](https://github.com/link-foundation/js-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template) and inherits its CI/CD jobs. The current `src/` is a placeholder; feature implementation will land milestone-by-milestone per [`solution-plan.md`](docs/case-studies/issue-1/solution-plan.md).\n\n## Status\n\n- **0.0.0**: scaffolding inherited from the JS template.\n- **0.0.1 (in progress, this PR #2)**: case study, requirements, solution plan, repository identity.\n- **0.0.2 → 0.0.x**: storage skeleton, importer adapters, UI surfaces — see [Solution Plan](docs/case-studies/issue-1/solution-plan.md).\n\n## Inherited features (CI/CD baseline)\n\n- **Multi-runtime support**: Works with Bun, Node.js, and Deno\n- **Universal testing**: Uses [test-anywhere](https://github.com/link-foundation/test-anywhere) for cross-runtime tests\n- **Automated releases**: Changesets-based versioning with GitHub Actions\n- **Code quality**: ESLint + Prettier with pre-commit hooks via Husky\n- **Package manager agnostic**: Works with bun, npm, yarn, pnpm, and deno\n- **Broken link checks**: Automated link validation with [lychee](https://github.com/lycheeverse/lychee-action) and Web Archive fallback suggestions\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### Development\n\n```bash\n# Install dependencies\nbun install\n\n# Run tests\nbun test\n\n# Or with other runtimes:\nnpm test\ndeno test --allow-read\n\n# Lint code\nbun run lint\n\n# Format code\nbun run format\n\n# Check all (lint + format + file size)\nbun run check\n```\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\n.\n├── .changeset/           # Changeset configuration\n├── .github/workflows/    # GitHub Actions CI/CD\n├── .husky/               # Git hooks (pre-commit)\n├── examples/             # Usage examples\n├── scripts/              # Build and release scripts\n├── src/                  # Source code\n│   ├── index.js          # Main entry point\n│   └── index.d.ts        # TypeScript definitions\n├── tests/                # Test files\n├── .eslintrc.js          # ESLint configuration\n├── .prettierrc           # Prettier configuration\n├── bunfig.toml           # Bun configuration\n├── deno.json             # Deno configuration\n└── package.json          # Node.js package manifest\n```\n\n## Design Choices\n\n### Multi-Runtime Support\n\nThis template is designed to work seamlessly with all major JavaScript runtimes:\n\n- **Bun**: Primary runtime with highest performance, uses native test support (`bun test`)\n- **Node.js**: Alternative runtime, uses built-in test runner (`node --test`)\n- **Deno**: Secure runtime with built-in TypeScript support (`deno test`)\n\nThe [test-anywhere](https://github.com/link-foundation/test-anywhere) framework provides a unified testing API that works identically across all runtimes.\n\n### Package Manager Agnostic\n\nWhile `package.json` is the source of truth for dependencies, the template supports:\n\n- **bun**: Primary choice, uses `bun.lockb`\n- **npm**: Uses `package-lock.json`\n- **yarn**: Uses `yarn.lock`\n- **pnpm**: Uses `pnpm-lock.yaml`\n- **deno**: Uses `deno.json` for configuration\n\nNote: `package-lock.json` is not committed by default to allow any package manager.\n\n### Code Quality\n\n- **ESLint**: Configured with recommended rules + Prettier integration\n- **Prettier**: Consistent code formatting\n- **Husky + lint-staged**: Pre-commit hooks ensure code quality\n- **File size limit**: Files must stay under 1500 lines for maintainability (enforced via ESLint and CI)\n\n### Release Workflow\n\nThe release workflow uses [Changesets](https://github.com/changesets/changesets) for version management:\n\n1. **Creating a changeset**: Run `bun run changeset` to document changes\n2. **PR validation**: CI checks for valid changeset in each PR\n3. **Automated versioning**: Merging to `main` triggers version bump\n4. **npm publishing**: Automated via OIDC trusted publishing (no tokens needed)\n5. **GitHub releases**: Auto-created with formatted release notes\n\n#### Manual Releases\n\nTwo manual release modes are available via GitHub Actions:\n\n- **Instant release**: Immediately bump version and publish\n- **Changeset PR**: Create a PR with changeset for review\n\n### CI/CD Pipeline\n\nThe GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/release.yml`) implements a fast-fail pipeline:\n\n**Fast checks** (~7-30s each, run first for fastest feedback):\n\n1. **Test compilation**: Syntax-checks all `.mjs` files with `node --check`\n2. **Lint, format & secrets scan**: ESLint, Prettier, jscpd, and [secretlint](https://github.com/secretlint/secretlint) for credential leak detection\n3. **File line limits**: Enforces 1500-line limit on `.mjs` files and `release.yml`\n4. **Changeset check**: Validates PR has exactly one changeset (added by that PR)\n5. **Version check**: Blocks manual version changes in `package.json`\n6. **Documentation validation**: Checks doc file sizes and required files\n\n**Slow checks** (only run after all fast checks pass):\n\n7. **Test matrix**: 3 runtimes × 3 OS = 9 test combinations\n8. **Broken link checks**: Validates all links in Markdown/HTML files (separate workflow)\n\n**Release** (on merge to main):\n\n9. **Changeset merge**: Combines multiple pending changesets at release time\n10. **Release**: Automated versioning and npm publishing\n\nSee [BEST-PRACTICES.md](docs/BEST-PRACTICES.md) for detailed explanations of each practice.\n\n#### Robust Changeset Handling\n\nThe CI/CD pipeline is designed to handle concurrent PRs gracefully:\n\n- **PR Validation**: Only validates changesets **added by the current PR**, not pre-existing ones from other merged PRs. This prevents false failures when multiple PRs merge before a release cycle completes.\n\n- **Release-time Merging**: If multiple changesets exist when releasing, they are automatically merged into a single changeset with:\n  - The highest version bump type (major > minor > patch)\n  - All descriptions preserved in chronological order\n\nThis design decouples PR validation from the need to pull changes from the default branch, reducing conflicts and ensuring that even if CI/CD fails, all unpublished changesets will still get published when the error is resolved.\n\n### Broken Link Checker\n\nThe link checker workflow (`.github/workflows/links.yml`) validates all links in Markdown and HTML files:\n\n1. **Detection**: Uses [lychee](https://github.com/lycheeverse/lychee-action) to scan all `*.md` and `*.html` files\n2. **Web Archive fallback**: For any broken links found, automatically checks the [Wayback Machine](https://web.archive.org) for archived versions\n3. **Actionable suggestions**: Reports one of three outcomes for each broken link:\n   - **Archived**: Suggests the Web Archive URL as a replacement\n   - **Not archived**: Clearly reports the link is unrecoverable\n4. **Scheduled checks**: Runs weekly to catch links that break over time (even if no files changed)\n5. **Issue creation**: On scheduled runs, creates a GitHub Issue with the full broken links report\n\nAdd regex patterns to `.lycheeignore` to exclude URLs from checks (e.g., local dev URLs, example.com, known rate-limited sites).\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Updating Package Name\n\nAfter creating a repository from this template, update the package name in:\n\n1. `package.json`: `\"name\": \"your-package-name\"`\n2. `.changeset/config.json`: Package references\n3. Scripts that reference the package name (see Quick Start)\n\n### ESLint Rules\n\nCustomize ESLint in `eslint.config.js`. Current configuration:\n\n- ES Modules support\n- Prettier integration\n- No console restrictions (common in CLI tools)\n- Strict equality enforcement\n- Async/await best practices\n- **Strict unused variables rule**: No exceptions - all unused variables, arguments, and caught errors must be removed (no `_` prefix exceptions)\n\n### Prettier Options\n\nConfigured in `.prettierrc`:\n\n- Single quotes\n- Semicolons\n- 2-space indentation\n- 80-character line width\n- ES5 trailing commas\n- LF line endings\n\n## Scripts Reference\n\n| Script                 | Description                             |\n| ---------------------- | --------------------------------------- |\n| `bun test`             | Run tests with Bun                      |\n| `bun run lint`         | Check code with ESLint                  |\n| `bun run lint:fix`     | Fix ESLint issues automatically         |\n| `bun run format`       | Format code with Prettier               |\n| `bun run format:check` | Check formatting without changing files |\n| `bun run check`        | Run all checks (lint + format)          |\n| `bun run changeset`    | Create a new changeset                  |\n\n## Contributing\n\nSee [CONTRIBUTING.md](docs/CONTRIBUTING.md) for detailed contribution guidelines.\n\nQuick steps:\n\n1. Fork the repository\n2. Create a feature branch: `git checkout -b feature/my-feature`\n3. Make your changes\n4. Create a changeset: `bun run changeset`\n5. Commit your changes (pre-commit hooks will run automatically)\n6. Push and create a Pull Request\n\n## Best Practices\n\nThis template implements CI/CD best practices for AI-driven development. See [BEST-PRACTICES.md](docs/BEST-PRACTICES.md) for details on:\n\n- File size limits for AI readability\n- Automated formatting and linting\n- Multi-runtime and cross-platform testing\n- Changeset-based versioning\n- Concurrency control for CI/CD pipelines\n\n## License\n\n[Unlicense](LICENSE) - Public Domain\n",

[2026-05-01T08:54:39.079Z] [INFO]     "originalFile": "# meta-sovereign\n\nA personal meta profile **sovereign** system. Fully local, privacy-respecting. The user actually owns and controls their data about their network of contacts, connections, and partners — across every social, messenger, and job-board network they use.\n\nTracked by issue [#1 — Prototype version 0.0.1](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-sovereign/issues/1). The full case study, requirements list, architecture sketch, and phased solution plan live in [`docs/case-studies/issue-1/`](docs/case-studies/issue-1/README.md).\n\n## What it will do (target capabilities)\n\n- **Unified inbox** spanning VK, Telegram, X, WhatsApp, Facebook, LinkedIn, career.habr.com, hh.ru, superjob.ru.\n- **Personal CRM**: contacts, communities, group memberships, intersections, mass-personal outreach.\n- **Personal memory**: structured `question → answer` facts captured automatically from conversations.\n- **Conversation automation platform**: pattern editors, reply-variation editors, n8n-style dialog graphs.\n- **Portable data store**: dual binary ([Doublets](https://github.com/linksplatform/doublets-rs)) + text ([Links Notation](https://github.com/link-foundation/links-notation)) representation, automated backups, `.lino` import/export.\n- **Local-first runtime**: WebRTC sync between user-owned devices, optional self-hosted personal cloud.\n- **Two stacks**: JS + Rust/WebAssembly (default) and pure Rust (server/microservice variant). The on-disk format is shared.\n\nThis repository is built on top of [`link-foundation/js-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template`](https://github.com/link-foundation/js-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template) and inherits its CI/CD jobs. The current `src/` is a placeholder; feature implementation will land milestone-by-milestone per [`solution-plan.md`](docs/case-studies/issue-1/solution-plan.md).\n\n## Status\n\n- **0.0.0**: scaffolding inherited from the JS template.\n- **0.0.1 (in progress, this PR #2)**: case study, requirements, solution plan, repository identity.\n- **0.0.2 → 0.0.x**: storage skeleton, importer adapters, UI surfaces — see [Solution Plan](docs/case-studies/issue-1/solution-plan.md).\n\n## Inherited features (CI/CD baseline)\n\n- **Multi-runtime support**: Works with Bun, Node.js, and Deno\n- **Universal testing**: Uses [test-anywhere](https://github.com/link-foundation/test-anywhere) for cross-runtime tests\n- **Automated releases**: Changesets-based versioning with GitHub Actions\n- **Code quality**: ESLint + Prettier with pre-commit hooks via Husky\n- **Package manager agnostic**: Works with bun, npm, yarn, pnpm, and deno\n- **Broken link checks**: Automated link validation with [lychee](https://github.com/lycheeverse/lychee-action) and Web Archive fallback suggestions\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### Development\n\n```bash\n# Install dependencies\nbun install\n\n# Run tests\nbun test\n\n# Or with other runtimes:\nnpm test\ndeno test --allow-read\n\n# Lint code\nbun run lint\n\n# Format code\nbun run format\n\n# Check all (lint + format + file size)\nbun run check\n```\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\n.\n├── .changeset/           # Changeset configuration\n├── .github/workflows/    # GitHub Actions CI/CD\n├── .husky/               # Git hooks (pre-commit)\n├── examples/             # Usage examples\n├── scripts/              # Build and release scripts\n├── src/                  # Source code\n│   ├── index.js          # Main entry point\n│   └── index.d.ts        # TypeScript definitions\n├── tests/                # Test files\n├── .eslintrc.js          # ESLint configuration\n├── .prettierrc           # Prettier configuration\n├── bunfig.toml           # Bun configuration\n├── deno.json             # Deno configuration\n└── package.json          # Node.js package manifest\n```\n\n## Design Choices\n\n### Multi-Runtime Support\n\nThis template is designed to work seamlessly with all major JavaScript runtimes:\n\n- **Bun**: Primary runtime with highest performance, uses native test support (`bun test`)\n- **Node.js**: Alternative runtime, uses built-in test runner (`node --test`)\n- **Deno**: Secure runtime with built-in TypeScript support (`deno test`)\n\nThe [test-anywhere](https://github.com/link-foundation/test-anywhere) framework provides a unified testing API that works identically across all runtimes.\n\n### Package Manager Agnostic\n\nWhile `package.json` is the source of truth for dependencies, the template supports:\n\n- **bun**: Primary choice, uses `bun.lockb`\n- **npm**: Uses `package-lock.json`\n- **yarn**: Uses `yarn.lock`\n- **pnpm**: Uses `pnpm-lock.yaml`\n- **deno**: Uses `deno.json` for configuration\n\nNote: `package-lock.json` is not committed by default to allow any package manager.\n\n### Code Quality\n\n- **ESLint**: Configured with recommended rules + Prettier integration\n- **Prettier**: Consistent code formatting\n- **Husky + lint-staged**: Pre-commit hooks ensure code quality\n- **File size limit**: Files must stay under 1500 lines for maintainability (enforced via ESLint and CI)\n\n### Release Workflow\n\nThe release workflow uses [Changesets](https://github.com/changesets/changesets) for version management:\n\n1. **Creating a changeset**: Run `bun run changeset` to document changes\n2. **PR validation**: CI checks for valid changeset in each PR\n3. **Automated versioning**: Merging to `main` triggers version bump\n4. **npm publishing**: Automated via OIDC trusted publishing (no tokens needed)\n5. **GitHub releases**: Auto-created with formatted release notes\n\n#### Manual Releases\n\nTwo manual release modes are available via GitHub Actions:\n\n- **Instant release**: Immediately bump version and publish\n- **Changeset PR**: Create a PR with changeset for review\n\n### CI/CD Pipeline\n\nThe GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/release.yml`) implements a fast-fail pipeline:\n\n**Fast checks** (~7-30s each, run first for fastest feedback):\n\n1. **Test compilation**: Syntax-checks all `.mjs` files with `node --check`\n2. **Lint, format & secrets scan**: ESLint, Prettier, jscpd, and [secretlint](https://github.com/secretlint/secretlint) for credential leak detection\n3. **File line limits**: Enforces 1500-line limit on `.mjs` files and `release.yml`\n4. **Changeset check**: Validates PR has exactly one changeset (added by that PR)\n5. **Version check**: Blocks manual version changes in `package.json`\n6. **Documentation validation**: Checks doc file sizes and required files\n\n**Slow checks** (only run after all fast checks pass):\n\n7. **Test matrix**: 3 runtimes × 3 OS = 9 test combinations\n8. **Broken link checks**: Validates all links in Markdown/HTML files (separate workflow)\n\n**Release** (on merge to main):\n\n9. **Changeset merge**: Combines multiple pending changesets at release time\n10. **Release**: Automated versioning and npm publishing\n\nSee [BEST-PRACTICES.md](docs/BEST-PRACTICES.md) for detailed explanations of each practice.\n\n#### Robust Changeset Handling\n\nThe CI/CD pipeline is designed to handle concurrent PRs gracefully:\n\n- **PR Validation**: Only validates changesets **added by the current PR**, not pre-existing ones from other merged PRs. This prevents false failures when multiple PRs merge before a release cycle completes.\n\n- **Release-time Merging**: If multiple changesets exist when releasing, they are automatically merged into a single changeset with:\n  - The highest version bump type (major > minor > patch)\n  - All descriptions preserved in chronological order\n\nThis design decouples PR validation from the need to pull changes from the default branch, reducing conflicts and ensuring that even if CI/CD fails, all unpublished changesets will still get published when the error is resolved.\n\n### Broken Link Checker\n\nThe link checker workflow (`.github/workflows/links.yml`) validates all links in Markdown and HTML files:\n\n1. **Detection**: Uses [lychee](https://github.com/lycheeverse/lychee-action) to scan all `*.md` and `*.html` files\n2. **Web Archive fallback**: For any broken links found, automatically checks the [Wayback Machine](https://web.archive.org) for archived versions\n3. **Actionable suggestions**: Reports one of three outcomes for each broken link:\n   - **Archived**: Suggests the Web Archive URL as a replacement\n   - **Not archived**: Clearly reports the link is unrecoverable\n4. **Scheduled checks**: Runs weekly to catch links that break over time (even if no files changed)\n5. **Issue creation**: On scheduled runs, creates a GitHub Issue with the full broken links report\n\nAdd regex patterns to `.lycheeignore` to exclude URLs from checks (e.g., local dev URLs, example.com, known rate-limited sites).\n\n## Configuration\n\n### ESLint Rules\n\nCustomize ESLint in `eslint.config.js`. Current configuration:\n\n- ES Modules support\n- Prettier integration\n- No console restrictions (common in CLI tools)\n- Strict equality enforcement\n- Async/await best practices\n- **Strict unused variables rule**: No exceptions - all unused variables, arguments, and caught errors must be removed (no `_` prefix exceptions)\n\n### Prettier Options\n\nConfigured in `.prettierrc`:\n\n- Single quotes\n- Semicolons\n- 2-space indentation\n- 80-character line width\n- ES5 trailing commas\n- LF line endings\n\n## Scripts Reference\n\n| Script                 | Description                             |\n| ---------------------- | --------------------------------------- |\n| `bun test`             | Run tests with Bun                      |\n| `bun run lint`         | Check code with ESLint                  |\n| `bun run lint:fix`     | Fix ESLint issues automatically         |\n| `bun run format`       | Format code with Prettier               |\n| `bun run format:check` | Check formatting without changing files |\n| `bun run check`        | Run all checks (lint + format)          |\n| `bun run changeset`    | Create a new changeset                  |\n\n## Contributing\n\nSee [CONTRIBUTING.md](docs/CONTRIBUTING.md) for detailed contribution guidelines.\n\nQuick steps:\n\n1. Fork the repository\n2. Create a feature branch: `git checkout -b feature/my-feature`\n3. Make your changes\n4. Create a changeset: `bun run changeset`\n5. Commit your changes (pre-commit hooks will run automatically)\n6. Push and create a Pull Request\n\n## Best Practices\n\nThis template implements CI/CD best practices for AI-driven development. See [BEST-PRACTICES.md](docs/BEST-PRACTICES.md) for details on:\n\n- File size limits for AI readability\n- Automated formatting and linting\n- Multi-runtime and cross-platform testing\n- Changeset-based versioning\n- Concurrency control for CI/CD pipelines\n\n## License\n\n[Unlicense](LICENSE) - Public Domain\n",

[2026-05-01T08:54:50.361Z] [INFO]     "originalFile": "# Contributing to js-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template\n\n## Development Workflow\n\n1. **Fork the repository** and clone your fork\n2. **Create a feature branch**: `git checkout -b feature/my-feature`\n3. **Install dependencies**: `bun install` (or `npm install`)\n4. **Make your changes**\n5. **Run local checks**: `bun run check`\n6. **Create a changeset**: `bun run changeset`\n7. **Commit and push** (pre-commit hooks will run automatically)\n8. **Create a Pull Request**\n\n## Code Standards\n\n### File Size Limits\n\n**Maximum 1500 lines per file** (enforced via ESLint `max-lines` rule and CI `check-file-line-limits` job).\n\nThis benefits both AI and human developers by ensuring files remain readable and maintainable.\n\n### Formatting and Linting\n\nAll code must pass:\n\n```bash\n# Check formatting\nbun run format:check\n\n# Check linting\nbun run lint\n\n# Run all checks\nbun run check\n```\n\nThe pre-commit hook runs automatically, but you can also manually format and fix:\n\n```bash\n# Auto-fix formatting\nbun run format\n\n# Auto-fix lint issues\nbun run lint:fix\n```\n\n### Testing Requirements\n\nTests should:\n\n- Cover critical paths\n- Work across all runtimes (Node.js, Bun, Deno)\n- Use the [test-anywhere](https://github.com/link-foundation/test-anywhere) framework\n\n```bash\n# Run tests\nbun test\nnpm test\ndeno test --allow-read\n```\n\n## Version Management with Changesets\n\nThis project uses [Changesets](https://github.com/changesets/changesets) to manage versions and changelogs. This eliminates merge conflicts that occur when multiple PRs bump the version in `package.json`.\n\n### Adding a Changeset\n\nWhen you make changes that affect users, add a changeset:\n\n```bash\nbun run changeset\n# or\nnpm run changeset\n```\n\nThis will prompt you to:\n\n1. Select the type of change (patch/minor/major)\n2. Provide a summary of the changes\n\nThe changeset will be saved as a markdown file in `.changeset/` and should be committed with your PR.\n\n### Changeset Guidelines\n\n| Type      | When to Use                          | Examples                                            |\n| --------- | ------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------- |\n| **Patch** | Bug fixes, internal changes          | Fix typo, update dependency, refactor internal code |\n| **Minor** | New features, non-breaking additions | Add new function, new optional parameter            |\n| **Major** | Breaking changes                     | Remove function, change API signature               |\n\nExample changeset summary:\n\n```markdown\nAdd support for custom configuration via config file\n```\n\n### What Changes Need Changesets?\n\n**Required** for:\n\n- Bug fixes\n- New features\n- Breaking changes\n- Public API changes\n\n**Not required** for:\n\n- Documentation-only changes (in `./docs` folder)\n- Changes to markdown files\n- CI/CD workflow updates (unless they affect users)\n\n## Release Process\n\nThe release process is fully automated:\n\n1. **PR with changeset merged** - The changeset is added to `.changeset/`\n2. **CI detects changesets** - On push to main, CI checks for pending changesets\n3. **Version bump** - Package version is updated based on changeset type\n4. **Changelog update** - `CHANGELOG.md` is updated automatically\n5. **npm publish** - Package is published via OIDC trusted publishing\n6. **GitHub Release** - A release is created with formatted notes\n\n### Multiple Changesets\n\nIf multiple PRs are merged before a release:\n\n- All changesets are merged into one\n- The highest version bump type wins (major > minor > patch)\n- All descriptions are preserved in chronological order\n\n## Pull Request Guidelines\n\n### PR Title\n\nUse a clear, descriptive title that summarizes the change:\n\n```\nfeat: Add support for custom configuration\nfix: Resolve race condition in async handler\ndocs: Update API documentation for v2\n```\n\n### PR Description\n\nInclude:\n\n- **What** - Describe the change\n- **Why** - Explain the motivation\n- **Testing** - How the change was tested\n\n### CI Checks\n\nAll PRs must pass:\n\n- [ ] Test compilation (syntax check)\n- [ ] Lint, format, and secrets scan\n- [ ] File line limits check\n- [ ] Changeset validation (for code changes)\n- [ ] Documentation validation (when docs change)\n- [ ] Tests on all platforms (Ubuntu, macOS, Windows)\n- [ ] Tests on all runtimes (Node.js, Bun, Deno)\n\n### Fresh Merge Simulation\n\nThe CI workflow automatically:\n\n1. Merges the latest `main` into your PR branch\n2. Runs checks against the merged state\n\nIf this fails with merge conflicts, you need to:\n\n```bash\ngit fetch origin main\ngit merge origin/main\n# Resolve conflicts\ngit push\n```\n\n## Questions?\n\nIf you have questions about contributing, feel free to open an issue for discussion.\n",

---------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| `README.md`            | Case-study `external-research.md` / `components.md`)\n\n- The link-foundation ecosystem already provides most of the storage primitives (`links-notation`, `lino-objects-codec`, `doublets-rs`, `doublets-web`, `lino-arguments`); the Universal Links Access layer can therefore be built mostly by integration, not from scratch.\n- Importers for VK and Telegram already exist as standalone konard repos and can be wrapped under a common `MessageSource` interface.\n- Beeper/Matrix bridges (`mautrix-*`) are the most legitimate path to a unified inbox spanning networks without violating ToS.\n- `automerge-repo` (structured data) + Yjs (free-text) is the recommended CRDT split for the WebRTC sync requirement.\n- `Rete.js v2` is the closest off-the-shelf match for the n8n-like automation graph (it ships an execution engine, not just a canvas).\n- Pattern editor: a small custom UI on top of `ohm-js` plus example-driven heuristics is the realistic prototype path; deeper synthesis (REGAE / SplitRegex) is a v0.x→v0.y research direction.\n\n## Out of scope (will be follow-up PRs)\n\n- Implementation of any importer adapter (VK, Telegram, etc.).\n- Any UI surface (chat, operator, automation graph, pattern editor).\n- WebRTC / CRDT sync layer.\n- Pure-Rust port of the server.\n\nThe full breakdown is in `docs/case-studies/issue-1/solution-plan.md` (Milestones 1–14). [2026-05-01T09:00:16.502Z] [STDOUT] ## Summary

Lands the documentation deliverable requested in issue #1 and the package metadata changes that establish the repository identity. No runtime feature code yet — feature implementation follows milestone-by-milestone in subsequent PRs per `docs/case-studies/issue-1/solution-plan.md`.

Closes the documentation requirements (R-I1..R-I5) of issue #1.

## What changed

### Repository identity

- `package.json` `name` set to `meta-sovereign` (was the template's `my-package`).
- `description`, `keywords`, and `repository.url` updated to match the project vision (Personal Meta Profile Sovereign system).
- `PACKAGE_NAME` constant updated in the changeset release scripts (`scripts/{validate,merge,publish,format-release-notes,create-manual}-changeset*.mjs`).
- `README.md` and `docs/CONTRIBUTING.md` updated to reflect the new project identity.

### Case study (`docs/case-studies/issue-1/`)

| File                   | Purpose                                                                                                                                |
| ---------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `README.md`            | Case-study overview, vision paraphrase, method, headline findings, constraints honoured.                                               |
| `requirements.md`      | 35 atomic requirements (`R-A1`..`R-I5`) extracted from the issue body, grouped by data layer, communication UI, pattern matching, CRM, external connectors, distribution, stack constraints, quality, documentation. |
| `components.md`        | Catalogue of every issue-linked repository (links-notation, lino-objects-codec, doublets-rs, doublets-web, link-cli, lino-arguments, deep-foundation/sdk, konard ingestion/broadcast tools, link-assistant/operator, browser-commander, JS/Rust pipeline templates). |
| `external-research.md` | Public-source survey: local-first software, CRDTs (Yjs/Automerge), Monica CRM, Beeper/Matrix bridges, regex/PEG synthesis, fuzzy search, node editors, WebRTC sync, messenger export formats, encryption-at-rest, browser SQLite, cross-platform shells. |
| `architecture.md`      | Layered architecture sketch, Universal Links Access API, `MessageSource` interface, dual-stack (JS+Rust/WASM and pure-Rust) parity track. |
| `solution-plan.md`     | 14 phased milestones mapping requirement IDs to concrete deliverables, with the definition of done for v0.0.1. |
| `data/`                | Raw issue body + comments captured via the `gh` CLI. |

### Headline findings (full reasoning in `external-research.md` / `components.md`)

- The link-foundation ecosystem already provides most of the storage primitives (`links-notation`, `lino-objects-codec`, `doublets-rs`, `doublets-web`, `lino-arguments`); the Universal Links Access layer can therefore be built mostly by integration, not from scratch.
- Importers for VK and Telegram already exist as standalone konard repos and can be wrapped under a common `MessageSource` interface.
- Beeper/Matrix bridges (`mautrix-*`) are the most legitimate path to a unified inbox spanning networks without violating ToS.
- `automerge-repo` (structured data) + Yjs (free-text) is the recommended CRDT split for the WebRTC sync requirement.
- `Rete.js v2` is the closest off-the-shelf match for the n8n-like automation graph (it ships an execution engine, not just a canvas).
- Pattern editor: a small custom UI on top of `ohm-js` plus example-driven heuristics is the realistic prototype path; deeper synthesis (REGAE / SplitRegex) is a v0.x→v0.y research direction.

## Out of scope (will be follow-up PRs)

- Implementation of any importer adapter (VK, Telegram, etc.).
- Any UI surface (chat, operator, automation graph, pattern editor).
- WebRTC / CRDT sync layer.
- Pure-Rust port of the server.

The full breakdown is in `docs/case-studies/issue-1/solution-plan.md` (Milestones 1–14). Each milestone references the requirement IDs it satisfies, so future PRs can cite e.g. _"R-A3: doublets+lino dual-store skeleton"_ and the requirements list stays a live spec.

## Test plan

- [x] `npm run format:check` passes (Prettier).
- [x] `npm run lint` passes (ESLint).
- [x] `npm test` passes (11/11 tests).
- [x] `.changeset/case-study-issue-1.md` describes the minor bump.
- [x] All commits are on `issue-1-fc41adad29ce`; branch pushed to remote.
- [x] No runtime code introduced — only documentation and metadata changes — so there are no new behaviour tests to add for this PR. Feature PRs from milestone 1 onward will ship unit + integration + e2e per R-H4.
